Summary: An assertion error can occur when generating RTL code with mixed NFP+HFP configuration
Description: When generating RTL code with mixed Native Floating Point (NFP) + Hard Floating Point (HFP) configuration, an assertion error can occur when these conditions are met: In the mixed NFP+HFP configuration, the vendor specific floating-point library is set to "ALTERAFPFUNCTIONS";? In the mixed NFP+HFP configuration, the NFP Latency Strategy is set to 'MIN'; The Simulink model contains Abs, Sign, or Unary Minus floating-point blocks?.  The assertion error message looks like:? ? ? Error using assert  ? ? Assertion failed.  ? ? Error in targetcodegen.nfpdriver/getDefaultLatency (line 231)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? assert(isa(hC, 'hdlcoder.abs_comp') || ...? ??  ? ? Error in targetcodegen.alterafpfdriver/getDefaultLatency (line 113)   ? ? ? ? ? ? ? [NFPLatency, ~] = getDefaultLatency@targetcodegen.nfpdriver(this, ...
Workaround: The assertion error can be avoided by one of these options:Use an NFP only configurationSet NFP Latency Strategy to "MAX"

Summary: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: javax/xml/ws/Holder exception displayed using Java Version 11 in R2023a
Description: When opening multiple files in MATLAB R2023a configured with Java Version 11 and switching to the "TILES" view by clicking on "Left/Right" under the "VIEW" menu, users may encounter the following issues: MATLAB? displays Java exception java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: javax/xml/ws/Holder ?with the detailed call stack. The content of the non-focused editor windows appears to be blank.?
Workaround: To work around the issue:Download the JAX-WS library from the following link: https://github.com/javaee/metro-jax-wsClick on "Download standalone distribution" in the README.md file and unzip the downloaded files to a local directory.After extracting the JAX-WS source files, navigate to the "lib" directory with the extracted files. Here you will find various JAR files.Identify the 'jaxws-api.jar' file within the "lib" directory, which needs to be added to classpath.txt.Open the "<matlabroot>/toolbox/local/classpath.txt" file using a text editor. (Replace the <matlabroot> with the MATLAB installed location)Add the following line to the 'classpath.txt' (Replace the <path_to_jaxws_downloaded_location> with the actual path to the directory to which you extracted the JAX-WS files in step 2.)? ? ? ? ? ?<path_to_jaxws_downloaded_location>/lib/jaxws-api.jar

Summary: Model Advisor check?Check signal flow in model?(Check ID:?mathworks.maab.db_0141) gives false negative result
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of Check?signal flow in model  ? (Check ID:?mathworks.maab.db_0141 )? does not report warnings when successive subsystems are not placed horizontally below the previous subsystem to form parallel subsystems.

Summary: MATLAB can crash at startup while initializing search functionality in help system
Description: MATLAB can crash at startup while initializing search functionality in help system. The stack trace might contain these lines: [? 0] 0x00007f0f7c122319 [unknown function] at /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+1434393 (no debugging symbols found)[? 1] 0x00007f0f7d2e7a88 foundation::core::process::context::context() at /matlab/foundation_libraries/src/fl/process/context.cpp:212[? 2] 0x00007f0ef79b25e0 docsearch::DocSearchServer::startupServer() at /matlab/toolbox/matlab/helptools/docsearch_server/startup_bundle/DocSearchServer.cpp:129[? 3] 0x00007f0ef79b32a9 docsearch::DocSearchServer::setup(startup_plugins::RuntimeOptions const&) at /matlab/toolbox/matlab/helptools/docsearch_server/startup_bundle/DocSearchServer.cpp:69

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect when a custom TLC storage class interacts with inlined S-function
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if asignal or state of a block downstream of inlined S-Function is? assignedto?a?storage classusing a custom TLC file , whereby?the contents  data access is requested where the set  access isexpected.? For example, get_data() ?might be generated on theleft-hand side of an assignment resulting in code such as get_data() =tmp;? instead of set_data(tmp); . The generated code might not compile depending on how the data access is implemented. A summary of the conditions are:? The model configuration parameter Enable local block outputs ?is selected Use of a storage class using a custom TLC file Use of an inlined S-Function with output set as local and reusable The storage class is not directly assigned to the S-Function output and is assigned downstream consisting of only data copy operations (that might be optimized)
Workaround: Option 1:Directly specify the storage class on the output of the inlined S-Function.Option 2:?Configure the output of the inlined S-Fuction as a test point.?Option 3:Force the output of the inlined S-Function to be global and non-reusable, usingssSetOutputPortOptimOpts(S, PortIndex,?SS_NOT_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL)Option 4:?Clear the model configuration parameter Enable local block outputs.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Simulink identifiers (SIDs) for blocks in referenced subsystems
Description: While a Subsystem Reference block is updating itself, Simulink might return incorrect Simulink identifiers with double colons (:: ) for the blocks in the referenced subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Automatic parallelization of for loops with reduction operations on floating-point types when nonfinite support is enabled
Description: The generated code is impacted by the issue when all  of the following conditions are satisfied:? The MATLAB code contains a for  loop that performs reduction operations on floating-point data. The configuration parameters Enable automatic parallelization? (EnableAutoParallelization ) and Optimize reductions  ( OptimizeReductions ) are both enabled.  The configuration parameter Support Non-finite   (SupportNonFinite ) is enabled.  In such situations, the code generator produces a parallel?for  loop that performs reduction operations on floating-point variables. These operations might generate nonfinite data during intermediate parallel execution. As a result, these operations might produce different results on subsequent runs depending on the order of execution of the threads.?This might cause the concurrent code to produce numerically incorrect results when compared to the results produced by the sequential code.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Disable the 'OptimizeReductions' configuration parameter. This prevents parallelization of any for loop that contains reduction operations, including arithmetic, logical, min, and max operations.If you can identify the specific loop causing the issue, you can disable parallelization for that loop by adding the coder.loop.parallelize('never') pragma immediately before the loop in your MATLAB code. For example:coder.loop.parallelize('never');for ii = 1:numel(arr1)    var = var + arr1(ii);endIf appropriate for your application, for standalone code generation (e.g., static libraries, dynamically linked libraries, or executable programs), disable the?Support Non-finite?(SupportNonFinite) code configuration parameter.

Summary: Display?block in referenced model does not display signal values during simulation when full path to block contains newline characters
Description: The Display block does not show signal values during simulation for a model where all of the following conditions are true: Contains a Model block that references a different model Specifies the full path to the Display block in that referenced model with newline characters
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Annotation connector comments for Simulink ports in the model do not show up in the generated HDL code
Description: Annotation comments associated with the ports in the Simulink model do not show up in the generated HDL code close to input and output ports in the entity or module declaration. HDL Coder only adds the annotation comments associated with Simulink blocks in the generated VHDL or Verilog code whereas the annotation comments for ports do not show up. Annotation comments help in mapping the algorithm in the Simulink model to the generated code and also improve readability and traceability of the code.
Workaround: Right-click on the port, choose port properties and use the Description: text box to enter the comment. The description text will be populated in the right place above the input or output port in the module or entity declaration.

Summary: Assertion failure when attempting to perform RAM Mapping on a non-scalar access
Description: When attempting to perform RAM Mapping on a non-scalar access, for example: A(1, 1:2) = uint32([4 5]); the following assertion failure might occur: Assertion Failed With Debug Message:B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\PromoteConditionalAccesses.cpp:53:qnt
Workaround: RAM Mapping is not supported for non-scalar accesses. Refactor the code so that variables that meet the RAM Mapping threshold are only accessed one element at a time.

Summary: too few arguments for call compilation error in atomic subsystem SIL/PIL simulations
Description: An atomic subsystem SIL/PIL simulation might fail to compile xil_interface.c , producing a too few arguments for call  error.The error occurs when?xil_interface.c  invokes a subsystem function, e.g. subsystem_init , with an incorrect number of arguments and?the function argument is a pointer to an enum  data? type.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to enumeration?function with string input
Description: For MATLAB code containing calls to the enumeration  function with a string input, the generated code returns an empty matrix instead of the contents of the enumeration. This bug does not affect calls to the enumeration  function? using character vector input.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef LightColors < uint8    enumeration        red(1)        yellow(2)        green(3)    endendfunction out = getColors()out = enumeration("LightColors");end? In MATLAB execution, calls to getColors  return the contents of the enumeration. If you generate a MEX for getColors , calls to getColors_mex  incorrectly return an empty matrix.? ?This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, replace string input to the?enumeration?function with character vector input.For example, in the code in the bug description, replace double-quoted input to the?enumeration?function ("LightColors") with single-quoted input ('LightColors').?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models with Specialized Power Systems blocks in Discrete mode
Description: When you simulate?Simscape Electrical?models that use blocks from the Specialized Power Systems library, MATLAB might crash if both  these conditions are true: You set the Simulation type  parameter of the Powergui block to Discrete. You select the Final States  and Save final operating point  parameters in the Data Import/Export ?pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Alternatively, at the MATLAB Command Window, you run the sim function with the SaveFinalState and SaveOperatingPoint?model configuration parameters set to "on".

Summary: Adding justification for the violations of the check?mathworks.hism.hisl_0011?does not work as expected
Description: When you add a justification to any one of the violations of an element for the check?Check usage of Switch Case blocks and Switch Case Action Subsystem blocks  (Check ID:?mathworks.hism.hisl_0011), it results in the addition of the same justification to all the violations of that element.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink fails to load struct input value to root-level inport in rapid accelerator mode simulations
Description: Simulink users can load data from the workspace to a root-level input port in the form of a structure . The name of the structure  is specified in the Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export > Input parameter . However, when the input is provided as a struct  with only one value, which corresponds to time zero, Simulink fails to load the provided value to the port during rapid accelerator mode simulation.
Workaround: The user can work around this issue by converting the data into a dataset.For example, if inputData?is the name of the struct to be used as input, use the following command to convert it into a dataset.?>>inputData = Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset(inputData)?

Summary: Bug Finder might compute HIS Comment Density as negative
Description: On some source files the HIS Comment Density "COMF" code metric is negative. This happens on files that contain multi-line preprocessing directives including comments.For example:  #if MACRO == 28 || MACRO == 156 /* comments*/ || MACRO == 29 || MACRO == 157 /*comments */ || MACRO == 31 || MACRO == 159 /* comments */ ||    MACRO == 32 || MACRO == 160 /* comments */ || MACRO == 7 || MACRO == 135 /* comments*/#endif
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Simulink might include incorrect header file in the generated code when multiple models share an imported enumeration type that has inconsistent?HeaderFile?property values across different models
Description: Simulink might include an incorrect header file in the generated code when all ?of the following are true: You configure more than one Simulink model use the shared utilities.  Multiple models use an enumeration type that has the DataScope ?property set to Imported .   Multiple models use different HeaderFile ?property values for the enumeration type.  The enumeration type is not used by any of the shared utilities.  The enumeration type is used in the generated files in the?model  folder.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: If the AWGN Block sample time is non-integer, the block generates noise with wrong variance
Description: Starting R2023a, you can update the AWGN block to the newer version using Model Advisor. The update calculates the samples per symbol as the ratio of symbol time and sample time. If the samples per symbol value is not an integer, it is rounded. This rounding causes the block to generate noise with a wrong variance value.

Summary: Unnecessary zero-crossing signal value type definition generated for referenced models
Description: Generated code for model references might include an unused type definition for the zero-crossing signal value even when all models in the model hierarchy disable fixed-step zero-crossing detection. The zero-crossing signal value type definition is not used if the top model does not enable fixed-step zero-crossing detection, even when the referenced model has fixed-step zero-crossing detection enabled.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Partially writing to a global or persistent variable on the GPU
Description: For MATLAB code that writes to a global or persistent variable, GPU Coder might generate incorrect code if all  these conditions are satisfied: The variable is partially but not fully written. For example, if the variable is a struct and only a single field is modified. The variable is modified because it appears as the output of a function call. The called function writes to the corresponding output parameter via a GPU kernel.  Example that results in incorrect code: function y = entryPoint??? persistent p;??? if isempty(p)??????? p.x = zeros(100);??????? p.y = 123;??? end??? % Problematic function call below??? p.x = foo;??? y = p.x(1) + p.y;endfunction Y = foo??? coder.inline('never');??? coder.gpu.kernelfun;??? Y = ones(100);end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing bus?signals that have array elements?with inplace update specifications
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a bus signal that has an array element. For example,  ? ? ? ?? ? ? ? ?struct {  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? int val[10];  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? }? BusA;  The elements of the bus signal are updated inplace by using one of these methods: Defining a MATLAB function interface in the form of y = foo(y) . Defining a?Reusable ?storage class and using the same variable name for the input and output of the update operation. Using function prototype control to specify the same name for the input and output of the update operation when the input and output of the update operation are the root input and output, respectively.   If your model satisfies the above conditions, for a defined bus X ? of type?BusA , the code generator might incorrectly?eliminate an assignment operation like the following: ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?X.val[index] = X.val[index -1];  // For arbitrary index values.

Summary: Incorrect 802.11be LDPC tone mapping distance for MRU size 52+26 is used in 802.11be waveform generation and reception
Description: When you generate or receive an 802.11be waveform for an MRU of size 52+26 using LDPC FEC coding, the waveform is generated or received incorrectly. This is due to an incorrect LDPC tone mapping distance for MRU size 52+26. The LDPC tone mapping distances for each RU or MRU size within an 80 MHz frequency subblock are defined in Table 36-52 in IEEE P802.11be/D3.0, January 2023.
Workaround: Change the value assigned to the variable?mappingDistance for MRU size 52+26 in wlan.internal.ehtLDPCToneMappingIndices?as follows:Open the file for editing:open wlan.internal.ehtLDPCToneMappingIndicesUpdate mappingDistance value for MRU size 52+26:With DCM, change mappingDistance to 3.Without DCM, change mappingDistance to 4.

Summary: C++ compiler issues warnings for narrowing conversions in coder_array.h file
Description: C++ compiler might issue warnings for narrowing conversions when the coder::array  type is instantiated with the bool  type instead of boolean_T .? This can happen under one of these circumstances:? The built-in C++ type bool  is used as boolean_T .  Custom C code explicitly instantiates coder::array  with the C++ bool  type. Calling a toolbox library (e.g. robotics) that uses coder::array  with the C++ bool  type.  The C++ compiler is unable to distinguish the constructor calls in a reshape ?method defined in coder_array.h because C++ treats pointer expressions as boolean  expressions. As a result, the use of the std::initialize_list  cannot choose the correct constructor call.
Workaround: If you have access to your MATLAB installation, go to your MATLABROOT and navigate to external header files for coder_array.h.?> cd (matlabroot)?>?cd extern/include/coder/coder_arrayOpen file coder_array_ rtw_cpp11.h?and search for the word reshaped. You will get this line:?? ? ? ? array_base<T, SZ, static_cast<SZ>(N1)> reshaped{const_cast<T*>(&data_[0]), _ns};?Modify the line to use parentheses instead of curly braces.? ? ? ? array_base<T, SZ, static_cast<SZ>(N1)> reshaped(const_cast<T*>(&data_[0]), _ns);?Save the file (select 'Overwrite' if prompted), restart MATLAB, and regenerate the code.

Summary: Error during mexcuda?compilation when only Visual Studio 2022 is available
Description: mexcuda  might ?error when Visual Studio 2022 is installed and no prior versions of Visual Studio (2017 or 2019) are available, displaying this error message: Did not find installed compiler 'NVIDIA CUDA Compiler' .
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP file?VisualStudio2022XML.zip?and extract its contents. The ZIP file contains the XML files nvcc_msvcpp2022.xml and nvcc_msvcpp2022_dynamic.xml. Override the default compiler selection using the?-f?filepath option of?mexcuda, specifying one of extracted XML files. If you are using the -dynamic option of mexcuda specify?nvcc_msvcpp2022_dynamic.xml, otherwise use?nvcc_msvcpp2022.xml.For example, to compile mexGPUExample.cu?using nvcc_msvcpp2022.xml?located under 'C:\Users\myusername\Downloads\', run this command:mexcuda mexGPUExample.cu -f 'C:\Users\myusername\Downloads\nvcc_msvcpp2022.xml'?

Description: MATLAB crashes when using gpuArray/arrayfun  if all of the following conditions are true: The function applied by arrayfun ? contains a call to a nested or external function on the path. The nested or external function uses one of?log , log1p , log10 , log2 , gammaln , reallog ,atanh , asin , acos , acosh , acoth , acsc , asec , asech , realsqrt , sqrt  The nested or external function uses one of rand , randi , randn . Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect optimization of uninitialized variables in MATLAB Function Block
Description: The code generator has an optimization capability to search for expressions that produce the same value at runtime and replace them with a single variable holding the computed value. For some MATLAB coding patterns, the code generator can incorrectly apply this optimization to code lines containing?variables not initialized in one of the possible paths, which leads to incorrect results. Consider the following MATLAB code in a MATLAB Function Block: function [x,y] = foo()  ? ? x = single(1);  ? ? % y = single(1); % with this line, the generated code is correct  ? ? for i = single(1):single(10)  ? ? ? ? y = x;  ? ? ? ? x = x + single(1);  ? ? end  end  The code contains a variable y , which is considered not initialized in one of the possible paths. At runtime, y  is never used without initialization because the for  loop is always executed. However, the optimization does not recognize this because the optimization assumes that any path of any branch can be executed. In the path where the for  loop is not executed, y  is used without initialization. Note that once the initialization of y  is added before the for  loop, the generated code is correct.? The generated C code for the MATLAB Function Block: ? x = 1;  ? for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {  ? ? x++;  ? }  ? Out1 = (real32_T)x;  ? Out2 = (real32_T)x;?  The code generator incorrectly sets?x(Out1)  and y(Out2) ?to the same value at the end of the function, which affects the program results.?? Note that both the MATLAB and Simulink workflows can trigger the bug.??

Summary: MATLAB is frozen when user tries to open the library file.
Description: Consider there are two libraries, library1 and library2 and a block is moved from library2 to library1. There is circular reference when library1 is opened after moving the block for these settings: The link for moved block in library1 is disabled and the disabled link (or AncestorBlock parameter) still provides reference to library1. Replacing the block in library2 with block from library1 creates active link to library2.  Operation performed: library2/block? ? ? ? -move->? ? ? library1  library2/block? ? ? ? -move->? ? ? library1/block (disable link)?  library2/block? ?<-replace-with-? ?library1/block (disabled link)   Result:? library2/block? ? -active-link-to->? ? library1/block  library1/block? ?-inactive-link-to->? ?library2/block  The go to library functionality shows the circular reference for these blocks. This circular reference causes MATLAB to freeze in infinite loop.
Workaround: Perform the break link operation on the moved block in library1 and then open library2 to replace the block with instance from library1. The recommended workflow to move a block from one library to another library is to break the link for the block after moving it to new library.library2/block? ?-move->? ?library1/block (break link)

The library method 'vision.internal.buildable.kdtreeBuildable.updateBuildInfo' failed.Caused by:    Unable to determine MEX compiler: use mex -setup to configure your system.

Summary: Error during update diagram or simulation when a referenced model contains Initialize Function block?connected to inactive root outport due to variants
Description: Simulink model containing a model block may throw an error during update diagram or simulation when the referenced model contains Initialize Function  block connected to root outport and can become inactive due to variant source/sink. Sample error message seen:? "Error:The Outport block <blockPath>' must be virtual. However, Simulink determined that a signal buffer is required at some point along the path from the source output port. Ensure that the connection from the source output port to the Outport block is a straight full port connection without any element reordering or selection. This message is related to a hidden Outport block.  Caused by:  ? ? The Outport block <block>' must be virtual because its source signal originates from an Initialize, Terminate, Reinitialize or Reset Function block. This message is related to a hidden Outport block.
Workaround: Ensure that the Outport block?originating from an Initialize Function block within the referenced model does not have any variant condition.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Array indexing might be incorrect for inplace MATLAB functions that accept variable-size arrays as inputs
Description: Incorrect code might generate from a MATLAB function when all of the following conditions are true: The MATLAB function is an inplace function (that is, uses the same input and output variable) whose input is a variable-size array. Inside the MATLAB function, an inplace operation changes the size of the input/output variable. The code in the MATLAB function performs some inplace operations on the input/output variable. You generate code that uses the row-major array layout. You generate code that uses N-dimensional indexing by setting the?PreserveArrayDimensions ?property to  true .  In R2021b or later,  EnableImplicitExpansion  is turned off.  Consider this MATLAB function that uses the same input and output variable x  and accepts a variable-size array as an input. The size of x ?is changed inside of the function through an inplace operation. function x = foo(x)? ? ? ? % In-place function with variable-sized input/output x = x(1,4:1:5);? ? ? ? ? ? % The size of x is changed  x = x + (x + ones(1,2));? ?% Inplace operation  end   When you configure the code generator to use row-major array layout and N-dimensional indexing,?you may see these code lines in the generated code: x_data[0] += x_data[0] + 1.0;  x_data[1] = x_data[0] + (x_data[0] + 1.0);   The generated code calculates the value assigned to? x_data[1] ?using?x_data[0] ?incorrectly.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Inactive variant of Variant Subsystem block containing Subsystem Reference block with loggedsignals might crash Simulink
Description: Simulink might crash when you simulate a Variant Subsystemblock with an inactive variant that has a Subsystem Reference block with? these conditions: The inactive variant has a Subsystem Reference block. The Subsystem Reference block has logged signals. The Subsystem Reference block is not updated.  The crash log displays the followinginformation:? [ 0] SleCompilerData::getActSrcs  [ 1] HMICompiler::Impl::isActualSourceValid  [ 2] HMICompiler::Impl::handleUnloggableSignals  [ 3] HMICompiler::compile  [ 4] SLCompPropagation  [ 5] run_compile_stage_handler  [ 6] sleCompileBlockDiagram  [ 7] sleCompileBlockDiagramWrapper  [ 8] SLCompDiagramImpl
Workaround: Update the Subsystem Reference block before you update the model diagram. To update the Subsystem Reference block:Open the subsystem containing the Subsystem Referenceblock in the Model hierarchy.Use the find_system, get_param, or set_paramcommands for?the Subsystem Reference block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated generic C/C++ code for deep learning networks produces incorrect results for some hardware targets
Description: Generated code for deep learning networks might produce incorrect results if?all  the following conditions are true: Target library is set to?'none'   The Device vendor  and Device type ?(ProdHWDeviceType ) ?configuration parameters are not set to any of the following values: "ARM Compatible->ARM Cortex "? "ARM Compatible->ARM Cortex-A " "ARM Compatible->ARM Cortex-M " "Intel->x86-64 (Linux 64) " "Intel->x86-64 (Mac OS X) " "Intel->x86-64 (Windows64 ) " "Generic->MATLAB Host Computer "   The Leverage target hardware instruction set extensions ?(InstructionSetExtensions ) ?configuration parameter is set to any value other than " None" ?or  the Code replacement library ?(CodeReplacementLibrary ) ?configuration parameter is set to "GCC ARM Cortex-A"
Workaround: Set both?Leverage target hardware instruction set extensions?(InstructionSetExtensions) and?Code replacement library?(CodeReplacementLibrary)?configuration parameters to "None".

Summary: PDF text extraction drops rotated text
Description: The?extractFileText ?function does not detect rotated text in PDF files. This can result in extractFileText ?extracting little to no text for affected pages.? Note that some PDF viewers automatically rotate pages so the text appears upright.

Summary: Model Maintainability Dashboard and metrics might return incorrect results for Simulink decision count and Simulink cyclomatic complexity
Description: The Model Maintainability Dashboard and metric results for the metrics?slcomp.SimulinkDecisions , slcomp.SimulinkDecisionDistribution , and?slcomp.SLCyclomaticComplexity ?might return values that are too low? because the metrics do not include Simulink decisions from certain block types.

Summary: Incorrect Accelerator mode simulation results when Stateflow chart contains a Parameter Writer block
Description: When you use?Accelerator  mode to simulate a model with the Parameter Writer block in a Stateflow chart, the simulation results are not correct because Simulink does not execute the Parameter Writer block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Binary operations might incorrectly expand singleton dimensions of dlarray operands
Description: The generated code might produce an incorrectly sized dlarray ?output,?if all  these conditions are true: A binary operation such as +, -, .*, etc. is performed on input?dlarray ?objects  For this operation, one operand is a formatted dlarray  and the other is an unformatted dlarray  The unformatted dlarray  operand was previously permuted  The formatted  dlarray ?operand has singleton dimensions? The corresponding dimensions in the unformatted  dlarray  operand are non-singleton   The following example produces a 1 x 4 x 5 x 4 shaped dlarray  instead of a 1 x 1 x 5 x 4 shaped dlarray . out = permute(dlarray(ones(1,4,1,1)),[3 4 1 2]) + dlarray(ones(1,1,5,4),'SSCB');?
Workaround: Extract the dlarray?objects before performing binary operations.For example, modify the code in the bug description as follows:out = extractdata(permute(dlarray(ones(1,4,1,1)),[3 4 1 2])) + extractdata(dlarray(ones(1,1,5,4),'SSCB'));?out = dlarray(out, 'SSCB');?

Summary: Error when running SIL/PIL simulation of subsystem within context of parent model that is mapped to AUTOSAR component
Description: When you run a SIL or PIL simulation of a subsystem within the context of a parent model that is mapped to an AUTOSAR component, you might observe this type of error: Unable to run SIL or PIL simulation for subsystem pathToSubsystem .?This error can occur if parent model cannot be compiled or subsystem contains blocks that are not supported for SIL and PIL simulations. For example, subsystem contains a Simulink Function block that is called by a Stateflow chart. ?  The error can occur when non-built-in data types, e.g. an enumeration, are present at the subsystem interface and the header file for the data type is not set to Rte_Type.h .
Workaround: For the non-built-in data types used at the subsystem interface, set the header file field to Rte_Type.h. You can use this approach even if the data types are internal and not used at the interface of the AUTOSAR component.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL test bench generation from a MATLAB function using the frame-to-sample optimization can be incorrect if the function has a scalar top-level input
Description: When generating HDL code from a MATLAB function, HDL Coder can generate an incorrect test bench if you use the frame-to-sample conversion optimization and have a scalar top-level input.

Summary: Using readRegister returns inaccurate data from the internal registers of an I2C peripheral device connected to an Aardvark or NI-845x controller
Description: An I2C transaction for a register read operation is expected to have only one stop condition at the end of the operation, after it reads data from the registers. However, the readRegister  function adds an extra stop condition after it writes the register address to read from, which can cause some I2C peripheral devices to return inaccurate data.

Summary: ModelAdvisor.run command gives incorrect counts of System statuses in Model Advisor Command-Line Summary
Description: The ModelAdvisor.run  command gives incorrect counts of the System statuses in the Model Advisor Command-Line Summary.

Summary: Creating a USB webcam object or changing the webcam resolution might crash MATLAB on Windows
Description: Creating a USB webcam object or adjusting the object resolution to a lower setting, might cause MATLAB to crash on Windows for some webcams. For example, the following lines of code can crash MATLAB. camObj = webcam; camObj.Resolution = '320x180';

Summary: Issues validating checks with updated Input Parameters
Description: When you use Model Advisor configuration files containing checks with updated Input Parameters , the JSON configuration files become corrupted, and the System tests do not run.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for A(idx,:) = []?does not remove the intended elements if?A is N-D array (N>2) and idx is logical column vector or matrix
Description: The generated code does not remove the intended elements from the array?A ?if all  ?these conditions are true:? Your MATLAB code contains patterns like A(idx,:) = [] ?or ?A(idx,:,:) = [] .  A  is an array of dimension greater than or equal to 3. idx  is a logical array. idx  is either a column vector or a matrix. (This bug does not occur if idx  is a row vector.)  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Convert the logical array idx?to a row vector.For example, replace the code line?A(idx,:) = []?with the following two lines:?idx2 = reshape(idx,1,[]);A(idx2,:) = [];
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Error during HDL code generation when a model has the configuration parameter?ReferenceDesignParameter set to a column of a cell array
Description: If you set the model configuration parameter?ReferenceDesignParameter  to a column vector of a cell array as shown below: ??hdlset_param(bdroot,'ReferenceDesignParameter',[{'MW_AXIS_DATA_WIDTH'; '32'}])  The following error occurs when generating HDL: Error using horzcat  Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent.
Workaround: To fix the error, use a row vector of values for ReferenceDesignParameter as shown below:?hdlset_param(bdroot,'ReferenceDesignParameter',[{'MW_AXIS_DATA_WIDTH', '32'}])

Summary: Inactive choices of a VariantSubsystem are built with inherited update diagram?activation time with?Model Reference?as choices
Description: The inactive choices within a Variant Subsystem get built when each of the following holds true:The Variant Activation time  of the Variant Subsystem is inherit from Simulink.VariantControl . The Variant Subsystem block has its choices as Model Reference  blocks. The ActivationTime  of the corresponding Simulink.VariantControl is update diagram .  You may also notice additional folders within the slprj  directory, corresponding to the inactive choices.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for dataflow subsystems configured for concurrent tasking with a user specified stack size
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that contains a Dataflow Subsystem block and meets these conditions: The configuration parameter Allow tasks to execute concurrently on targets  is selected. The Maximum stack size (bytes)  value is specified.  In the generated code, some local variables might be promoted to global variables to comply with the stack size limit. If these global variables are accessed concurrently by multiple threads, it can potentially lead to race conditions.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, increase the stack size value or do not specify a stack size in Maximum stack size (bytes) field.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Error generating web views for AUTOSAR architectures
Description: When generating a web view for AUTOSAR architecture models using Simulink(R) Report Generator(TM), the following error is encountered:  Checking for links ...No links.Warning: Malformed?JSON file.Error while evaluating TimerFcn for timer 'timer-1'Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 0.?

Summary: Computer restarts when configuring or loading Simulink Desktop Real-Time hardware driver
Description: A Windows computer that has the Core Isolation Memory Integrity feature enabled might restart when you configure or load a Simulink Desktop Real-Time hardware driver.? For example, you configure and load a hardware driver when you open the Board Setup  dialog box and click the Test  button.

Summary: Model Testing, Model Design, or Code Quality Dashboards might error for project saved in previous release
Description: Opening the Model Testing, Model Design, or Code Quality Dashboard on a project might error if the project was saved in a previous release and you are on a Mac or Linux machine. An error dialog in the dashboard shows the error:? Unable to initializeservice 'ArtifactService': fl:filesystem:SystemError?  The dashboard remains empty.
Workaround: Delete or rename the dashboard database files in the project.Close the project and any open dashboards.Navigate to the root of the project.In the derived folder, delete* or rename these two files:artifacts.dmrresultservice.dmrRe-open the project and dashboard.*Note:?If you delete the files, you lose data, including traceability information, that the dashboard collected and can still open in the previous release.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Realtime targets with startup variant blocks can cause incorrect code generation with page switching storage class
Description: The default external parameters behavior 'PageSwitching(slrealtime) ' of Simulink Real-Time targets may cause incorrect code generation for startup variant blocks where the generated code may contain temporary variables which are read before initialization. For example:? void step()?  {?  ? ? real_T tmp;?? ? if (tmp == 1.0 || tmp == 2.0 || tmp == 3.0) {? ? ? ? ?tmp = *get_V();? ? ?}?  ? ? ?if (tmp == 1.0) {?? ? ? ? code for variant choice1;? ? ? } else if (tmp == 2.0) {? ? ? ? ?code for variant choice2;? ? ? ?} else if (tmp == 3.0) {?? ? ? ? code for variant choice3;?? ? ?}  ? ? ? ...?}  Here the temporary variable tmp  ? is read before initialization causing the incorrect code.
Workaround: Change the PageSwitching(slrealtime) in? Code mappings ->?Data Defaults -> External Parameters to other available options such as ExportedGlobal or ImportedExtern.?

Summary: Loading an architecture model with an outdated profile where a stereotype was renamed and re-parented can cause a crash
Description: MATLAB might crash during loading an architecture model when all the following conditions are met: The model is associated with an outdated profile. The outdated profile contains a stereotype that was both renamed and re-parented. A re-parented stereotype inherits from a different base stereotype.

Summary: Performance of mtimes has regressed on NVIDIA Ampere architectures GPUs
Description: The performance of mtimes  with gpuArray  double or complex double inputs has regressed on NVIDIA Ampere architectures GPUs. The scale of the regression depends on the type of the inputs (for example, double complex) and the dimensions of the inputs, defined as follows: m ?is the number of rows of the first input. n ?is the number of columns of the second input. p ?is the number of columns of the first input and number of rows of the second input.?  Case 1:  m*n*p <= 2097152 && m > 1 && n > 1   The performance regression is most severe for double and complex double inputs when?m*n*p <= 2097152 && m > 1 && n >> 1 . ?  For example, on an NVIDIA A100 GPU, the mtimes ?call in the following code is about 28x slower than in the previous release: m = 2;n = 6;p = 100000;A = rand(m,p,"gpuArray");?B = rand(p,n,"gpuArray");?t = gputimeit(@() A*B);  The approximate execution times are:? R2022b:? 83 microseconds R2023a:? 2400 microseconds Case 2:? m*n*p > 2097152   A smaller magnitude performance regression also exist for double inputs when m*n*p > 2097152 . For example, on an NVIDIA A100 GPU, the mtimes ?call in the following code is about 1.3x slower than in the previous release: m = 2;n = 2;p =?2000000;A = rand(m,p,"gpuArray");?B = rand(p,n,"gpuArray");?t = gputimeit(@() A*B);  The approximate execution times are:? R2022b: 460 microseconds R2023a: 610 microseconds Case 3:? m*n*p <= 2097152 && (m == 1 || n == 1)   Regressions also occur for double inputs when multiplying either a row vector by a matrix, or a matrix by a column vector. For example, on an NVIDIA A100 GPU, the?mtimes ?call in the following code is about 15x slower than in the previous release: m = 1;n = 20;p =?100000;A = rand(m,p,"gpuArray");?B = rand(p,n,"gpuArray");?t = gputimeit(@() A*B);  The approximate execution times are:? R2022b: 32 microseconds R2023a: 470 microseconds
Workaround: Case 1: m*n*p <= 2097152?If the inputs are such that?m*n*p <= 2097152, improve the performance by padding the inputs such that m*n*p >?2097152. For example,?on an NVIDIA A100 GPU, the matrix multiplication in the following code is approximately 9.6x faster when using the workaround:m = 2;n = 6;p = 100000;A = rand(m,p,"gpuArray");?B = rand(p,n,"gpuArray");tNoWorkaround = gputimeit(@() A*B);tWorkaround = gputimeit(@()?mtimesWorkaround(A,B));function C = mtimesWorkaround(A,B)?? ? m = size(A,1); ?? ? [p,n] = size(B);? ? if m*n*p <= 2097152 ?? ? ? ? paddedN = floor(2097152/(m*p)) + 1;? ? ? ? B(:,n+1:paddedN) = 0;? ? ? ? C = A*B;?? ? ? ? C = C(:,1:n);?? ? else?? ? ? ? C = A*B;? ? endendThe approximate execution times are:??No workaround: 2400 microseconds?Workaround: 250 microseconds?Case 2: m*n*p > 2097152?If the inputs are such that?m*n*p > 2097152, avoid the regression by setting the environment variable CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG to :4096:8 before starting MATLAB. ?This might negatively affect the performance of other MATLAB functions that run on the GPU.Case 3:?m*n*p <= 2097152 && (m == 1 || n == 1)If one of the inputs is a row vector or a column vector, and the other input is a matrix, improve the performance by transposing the matrix input before calling mtimes and then transpose it back as part of the mtimes expression. ?For example,?on an NVIDIA A100 GPU, the matrix multiplication in the following code is approximately 5.8x faster when using the workaround:m = 1;n = 20;p =?100000;A = rand(m,p,"gpuArray");?B = rand(p,n,"gpuArray");tNoWorkaround = gputimeit(@() A*B);tWorkaround = gputimeit(@()?A*(B.').');The approximate execution times are: ?No workaround: 470 microseconds?Workaround: 80 microseconds ?

Summary: Audio plugin generation fails to save plugins generated using Xcode 14.3
Description: When generating a plugin using Xcode 14.3, the output file is not found. For example, the code >> generateAudioPlugin audiopluginexample.Echo?  produces an error similar to this one: Error using generateAudioPluginCould not save plugin to '/tmp/Echo.vst': The location'/private/var/folders/fd/....../Builds/MacOSX/build/Release/Echo.vst' was not found.The location may be read-only or locked by a DAW process.?  The file exists in the ~/Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/  folder but cannot be copied to the output directory.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Use the -auv3 option or the?-exe option whenever possible.Copy the file from the plugin directory to the output directory. For example, if the plugin has the name?Echo:For? AU:>>?copyfile ~/Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/Components/Echo.component Echo.componentFor? VST:>>?copyfile ~/Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/VST/Echo.vst Echo.vstFor? VST3:>>?copyfile ~/Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/VST3/Echo.vst3 Echo.vst3

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unsupported export-function modeling patterns generate incorrect code instead of throwing an error
Description: The code generator incorrectly generates code instead of throwing an error when all ?of the following are true: Export-function code is being generated. A root inport is connected to a referenced model. The root inport has function access (for example, the GetSet  storage class). The referenced model signal line to which the root inport is connected to drives a Function-Call Subsystem and one or more Function-Call Subsystem, Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Functions.  For example, consider a top model, top , that includes a referenced model, bot . The referenced model has a reset function, reset , and a function call subsystem, fcn1 . A root inport, In1 , is connected to reset  and fcn1 . The code generator produces this code for the top model:? void top_reset(void)  {  ? bot_reset(&top_B.Access_In1_to_Model,?&top_Y.Out1[0]);  }  void fcn1(void)?  { ? ? bot_fcn1(&top_B.Access_In1_to_Model,?&top_Y.Out1[0]);  }   The generated functions do not assign a valid value to? top_B.Access_In1_to_Model  before passing it to the  bot_reset  and  bot_fcn1  functions. The generated function should assign a value by using an access function.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Correctly sequenced double writes cause false positive of AUTOSAR C++14 A5-0-1 and other standards
Description: AUTOSAR C++14 A5-0-1 specifies "The value of an expression shall be the same under any order of evaluation that the standard permits."Polyspace Bug Finder reports false positives on these kind of constructions:x = x = 0;  The false positive violation occurs for these rules as well: AUTOSAR C++14: A5-0-1 CERT C: EXP10-C, EXP30-C CERT C++: EXP50-CPP MISRA AC AGC: 12.2 MISRA C:2004: 12.2 MISRA C++:2008: 5-0-1 JSF AV: 204.1  Note that this violation remains a true positive violation for: MISRA C:2012: 13.2

Summary: resample?returns wrong output with a nonuniform N-dimensional signal and a time vector with?NaNs or NaTs
Description: The?(x,tx,_) ?syntax of the?resample  function returns wrong output when: The input signal x  is nonuniform x  has three dimensions or more The time vector tx  contains NaN s or NaT s  When these conditions are met, the output of resample  is a two-dimensional array with elements in the wrong order.

Summary: Meshing errors with certain STL files that have bad features
Description: A check for detecting closed surface meshes results in errors at the meshing stage if the STL file contained bad features.

Summary: Modifying subsystem reference from callback in block outside that subsystem reference produces an error
Description: If a block outside a Subsystem Reference block contains a callback that tries to make any changes to that subsystem, this error occurs: Unable to modify contents of a Subsystem Reference block '<blockname>'.  The ancestor blocks within Subsystem Reference are only allowed to modify the contents of block 'blockname'. "?

Summary: Incorrect Polygon label data after importing into the Image Labeler app.
Description: Incorrect representation of Polygon  label annotation data after importing labels or loading a project while using theImage Labeler  app. This issue occurs only for images labeled with a single Polygon  label annotation. The app displays the Polygon  label as a set of unconnectedpoints rather than a single polygon of connected vertices. The app then treats the unconnected points as individual Polygon  label annotations.

Summary: Variant Subsystems with Model Reference as the active choice, opens the referenced model in a new window on double click
Description: If the Simulink model contains a Variant Subsystem that has the Model Reference as the active choice, then double clicking on the Variant Subsystem will open up the referenced model in a new window. The expectation here is that the Model Reference will open in the same window as the Variant Subsystem.

Summary: Unreachable disable functions when referencing a model in a function-call subsystem
Description: The generated code for model reference hierarchies might contain unreachable disable  functions. For example, the generated code might contain unreachable?disable ?functions when all of the following conditions are true: A model contains a Switch Case Action Subsystem block. That model is referenced by a Model block in a function-call subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Handle object modified via multiple variables inside the same function
Description: This issue occurs if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions: In the same function, multiple variables (for example, x and?y ) refer to the same handle object.?For example,x = MyHandle;y = x; Instead of directly referring to the handle object,? x and y can also be classes, cell arrays, or structs that contain references to the same handle object.  A property p  of this handle object is assigned a value via the variable?x . ?This value contains some constants that are known during code generation. For example,x.p = 0  The same property p  of this object is then assigned a different value via the variable?y . For example, y.p = 1  The same property p  is then read using the variable?x . For example, use(x.p)   In such situations, the use of x.p ?might generate code that uses the original value assigned via?x.p , thereby ?ignoring the value assigned later via?y.p .
Workaround: In the above example, put?the constant value that is assigned to x.p inside the?coder.ignoreConst?function:x.p = coder.ignoreConst(0)

Summary: Sporadic delay balancing error when generating reusable code for subsystems that contain a feedback loop
Description: Delay balancing cam fail sporadically and throw an error when the subsystems to be reused contain a feedback loop.? This is the delay balancing error message that occurs: Delay balancing unsuccessful because the input and output configurations of this atomic subsystem cause delay balancing to fail. Consider making it non-atomic
Workaround: To avoid this error, follow these steps to make the subsystem non-atomic to exclude the subsystem from being reused:Follow the error message to find the subsystem being reusedRight-click on the subsystem and open Block ParametersIn the Main tab, uncheck Treat as atomic unit

Summary: Top-level model with multiple instances of same referenced model with a Parameter Writer block might produce wrong simulation results
Description: Simulation results might be wrong for a top-level model with multiple instances of the same referenced model when all of the following conditions are true: The referenced model has a Parameter Writer block that writes to a built-in block parameter, mask parameter, or Model block instance parameter. The Parameter Writer block is connected directly or indirectly to a root-level Inport block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Exporting a model with a Variant Assembly Subsystem block in expression mode to the R2022b version of MATLAB results in an unsupported exported model
Description: In R2022b, the Variant Assembly Subsystem  block only supports the label  variant control mode.?Exporting a model with a Variant Assembly Subsystem ?to the R2022b results in an unsupported exported model when: ?The model with Variant Assembly Subsystem  has Variant control mode  set to expression   Post a successful export to R2022b, you will notice Variant control mode  dialog to be disabled. Additionally, model simulation may error out with the below error message: Variant control 'mChoice1' of variant block 'mModel/vas/mChoice1' must be a condition expression or name of a Simulink.Variant object in the global scope workspace of the model. Ensure that the condition does not match the model name.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Playback block outport that has variable-sized signal outputs empty result at time 0 in Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator mode simulation
Description: The outport of a Playback block outputs a 0-by-0 matrix at time step 0 when all ?of the following are true:? The output signal of the Playback block is variable-sized The simulation is running in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode  This output is inconsistent with the result in Normal simulation mode and in the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL FIFO block might generate incorrect output values when the Reset and Pop signals are asserted at the same time.
Description: The simulation and generated HDL code for HDL FIFO block output incorrect values when you use the following settings for the block: Reset and Pop signals are asserted at the same time.? Duration of Reset signal is equal to one time-step.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Compilation error in C++ generated code due to the absence of namespace in C-API file
Description: Generated C++ code from a Simulink model that contains a referenced model or is configured to generate reusable code for the top model might produce a compilation error stating use of an undeclared identifier.  The error is due to the absence of a namespace in the second argument of the function model_InitializeDataMapInfo  in C-API file (model_capi.h ).

Summary: Hyperlinks in the Code Generation report do not work after the generated or validation model is closed
Description: After closing the generated and/or validation models, unlike the original models,  these models are removed from the memory.? Although the names and paths in the hyperlinks are correct, MATLAB cannot open these models. If you click on these hyperlinks, you get an error message that says: "cannot find such model"

Summary: Assertion error when sending events that could cause recursive execution from transitions connected to exit or entry ports
Description: If the effects of sending an event cause a transition path to terminate execution, and that transition path is connected to an exit or entry port, Stateflow might throw an assertion error. For example, if the sending of the event causes a different state to be active, early return logic will terminate the execution of that transition path.

Summary: Memory leak observed with MATLAB interface to a C++ library in outofprocess?Execution mode?
Description: If you use a MATLAB interface to a C++ library in outofprocess  Execution mode, you might observe a continuous increase in memory.??This happens as a result of a memory leak while calling C++ functions in outofprocess  mode.

Summary: Network latency mismatch between deep learning processor profiler and estimator results
Description: When using certain sizes of the fully-connected (FC) layer, the estimated FC layer latency may be around 400 cycles different from the profiler results. The total network latency may also differ depending on the number of FC layers used. For example, an LSTM that contains four FC layers could have a difference of 400 cycles per FC layer, leading to a total network latency difference of 1600 cycles."

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink parameter with non-Auto storage class might be incorrectly overwritten in initialize function when used to specify constant value of bus element signal connected to the outport of a conditionally-executed subsystem.
Description: The code generator might dynamically initialize a statically-initialized Simulink parameter when?all ?of the following are true: A Simulink.Parameter  is used as a constant value for a constant block. This Constant block?is connected as an input port of a Bus Creator block. The output port of the Bus Creator block is driving an Outport block of a conditionally-executed subsystem such as Enabled Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem. The parameter Source of initial output value  of the Outport block is set to 'Input signal' .  When the above conditions are met, Simulink might generate code similar to the following. /* Exported block parameters */  uint8_T myParam = 15U;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* Variable: myParam?  /* Model initialize function */  void simplify1_initialize(void)  {   /* SystemInitialize for Outport: '<S1>/Raw_Bytes' incorporates:   ?*? Constant: '<S2>/Const_dummy_Byte_8'   */   ?myParam = simplify1_ConstP.Raw_Bytes_Y0.Raw_Byte_8;?    }?   Note that the Simulink.Parameter  myParam   is statically initialized with value  15U , but it gets dynamically initialized? incorrectly?to some other value?inside the model initialize function.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Peripheral blocks inside model reference do not work during app-based build with Configure, Build and Deploy when using C2000 Microcontroller Blockset?
Description: When model is configured for Hardware Boards TI Delfino F2837xD, TI Delfino F2837xD Launchpad or TI F2838x, then Peripheral do not get initialized when peripheral blocks are used inside model references. Model builds successfully in this multicore workflow, when SoC Builder is launched using Configure, Build and Deploy, but peripherals do not work functionally due to issue with initialization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When using a storage class with?Data Access set to Function and Allowed Access set to Write-only, the generated write method ignores the identifier naming rule
Description: The code generator generates code that does not honor the identifier naming rule when all ?of the following are true: You use a storage class that has Data Access ?set to Function  That storage class also has Allowed Access ?set to Write-only   For example, consider a model that as an import testa , a Gain block that has a value of 2, and an outport  testb . The outport uses  Write-only ?access and the Name of Setter ?identifier naming rule is  Write-$N . The code generator produces this code:  ?set_rttestb_value(2.0 * get_rttesta());   The pseudocode?preview shows this set function: void Write_DATANAME(CONST DATATYPE DATANAME);   The pseudocode preview honors the identifier naming rule, but the generated code does not.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Bug Finder stops at the end of the Function Analysis with a segmentation fault
Description: A segmentation fault occurs at the end of the Bug Finder Function Analysis when all the following conditions exist: One or more Autosar functions (name starts with "Rte_ ") have names of more than 100 characters. These Autosar functions are called in the program being analyzed. The "-library autosar " option is used in the configuration of Bug Finder.
Workaround: Remove the "-library autosar" option from the configuration of Bug Finder.

Summary: Generated HDL code from a MATLAB Function block that has an output variable assigned from a persistent variable might have a compilation error
Description: The generated HDL code from a MATLAB Function block that has an output variable assigned the value of a persistent variable before the persistent variable is updated can be incorrect when the output appears on the right-hand side of an assignment. As a result, the incorrect generated code will not compile.?? For example, incorrectly generated HDL code and a subsequent?compilation error will occur if generate HDL code from this MATLAB code in a MATLAB Function block:  function out = fcn()  ??? persistent reg;  ??? if (isempty(reg))  ??????? reg = false;  ??? end  ???? out = reg;  ??? reg = ~ reg;   end
Workaround: Add a dummy variable to the MATLAB code in the MATLAB Function block to avoid a direct assignment from a persistent variable to an output variable. See this MATLAB code example:function out = fcn()??? persistent reg;??? persistent dummy;??? if (isempty(reg))??????? reg = false;??????? dummy = false;??? end???? out = or (reg, dummy);??? reg = ~reg;? ? dummy = false; end

Summary: Debugging Stateflow chart with dangling transitions causes MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you update or run a model containing a Stateflow chart where all of the following conditions are true: The chart contains a?dangling transition  You have set a breakpoint on any state or transition in the chart  The generated stack trace refers to sf_cdr.dll.
Workaround: Delete all dangling transitions. They are illegal and ignored by Stateflow.?

Summary: Error when generating code from a model with Simulink Function configured with return argument
Description: The code generation process might produce an error for a model that meets all ?of these conditions: The model contains a Simulink Function for which a customized C/C++  Function Interface is present with return argument as nonvoid data.? The Array layout  model configuration parameter is set to Row-major . At least one of the arguments for the Simulink Function is a matrix or array.  The error resembles this:  CGIR assertion 'In checkUntyped: Assertion failed: !UntypedType::isa(src)' failed in 'B:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_core\lowering\majorityLowering\MajorityLowerType.cpp:1132
Workaround: Change the Array layout model configuration parameter to?Column-major.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you try to simulate a model while the model is closing
Description: When you try to simulate a model while that model is closing, MATLAB crashes if the model is configured to simulate in normal or accelerator mode.  For example, this crash occurs if the CloseFcn ?callback for a model:  Calls the sim ?function  Uses this set_param ?command to start the simulation:? set_param(mdl,"SimulationCommand","start")   The code in the  CloseFcn ?callback function that tries to start a simulation might be part of a script or a function .  This crash does not occur for rapid accelerator simulations.

Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 M0-1-3 when mixing C source files and C++ source files in a project
Description: Polyspace incorrectly raises violations of AUTOSAR-C++14 M0-1-3 when a variable you declare in a header is used in a *.c  file but not used in a *.cpp  file. For example: // header.h extern int var1;   // Incorrectly raises AUTOSAR-C++14 M0-1-3extern int var2; // test.c#include "header.h" void funcC(void) {    var1 = 0;   var2 = 0; } // test.cpp#include "header.h"  void funcCPP(void) {   var2 = 1; }

Summary: MATLAB fails to authenticate SSH connections using RSA keys to remote machines running OpenSSH server 8.8 onwards
Description: When you authenticate an SSH connection to a remote machine with an identity file in the RSA format, MATLAB Parallel Server fails to establish the connection and produces an error like the following: Identity file not accepted by remote host?'machine32.mycompany.com'. Check the identity file is valid and has correct permissions.  Error from library: Username/PublicKey combination invalid  This error occurs when authenticating an SSH connection to a machine running OpenSSH server version 8.8 or later. The RSA private key might work when you authenticate using OpenSSH client on the command line. A private key is in RSA format if it begins with the following line: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY -----
Workaround: Do one of the following:Use a private key in a format other than RSA, such as Ed25519.Use a password to authenticate.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Negative linear velocity inputs produce an incorrect state derivative for?the bicycleKinematics object.
Description: When the  bicycleKinematics object is defined with the  VehicleInputs property set to " VehicleSpeedHeadingRate", the  derivative method returns incorrect state derivatives for negative linear velocity inputs.  This example shows the error: bk = bicycleKinematics(VehicleInputs="VehicleSpeedHeadingRate"); vehicleSpeed = -1;  omega = -1;?  cmds = [vehicleSpeed omega];?  stateDot = derivative(bk,[0 0 0],cmds) The expected output for  stateDot is:  [-1; 0; -1] The actual output for  stateDot is:  [-1; 0; 1] The third element of  stateDot, which corresponds to the angular velocity, is incorrect.
Workaround: Use the bicycleKinematics object with the VehicleInputs property set to "VehicleSpeedSteeringAngle". Then use this code to convert a vehicle speed and heading rate command to a vehicle speed and steering angle command:bk = bicycleKinematics(VehicleInputs="VehicleSpeedSteeringAngle");cmds = [vehicleSpeed omega];psi = atan2(cmds(2)*bk.WheelBase,cmds(1));% Wrap the steering angle psi to lie between -pi/2 and pi/2?if psi > pi/2? ? psi = psi-pi;elseif psi < -pi/2? ? psi = psi+pi;end% Package the commands in a vector input to the derivative method, then call the derivative method with the processed commands. The conversion to of heading rate to steering angle ensures that the max steering angle constraint can be correctly enforced.speedAndSteeringAngleRateCommands = [cmds(1) psi];stateDot = derivative(bk,[0 0 0],speedAndSteeringAngleRateCommands)

Summary: Reading the BLE descriptor data on macOS version 12.0 or above can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you?read the descriptor data?on a bluetooth low energy device?on macOS version 12.0 or above, MATLAB crashes. For example, the following code causes this crash: b = ble("device"); c = characteristic(b,"Service","Characteristic");d = descriptor(c,"Descriptor") read(d)
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A0-1-4
Description: Polyspaces raises an incorrect violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A0-1-4 when a parameter of reference type is used as the argument of the sizeof  operator. For example: #define UNUSED_PARAMETER(x) (void)(sizeof(x))void foo(int const& f) // Incorrect violation{   UNUSED_PARAMETER(f);}

Summary: Pool creation fails when using the 'Processes'parallel environment on a machine with no active network adapters
Description: When you start a parallel pool using the 'Processes ' parallel environment on a machine with no active network adapters, pool creation fails with with a message that refers to "The requested name is valid, but no data of the requested type was found ".  For example, this parpool command fails with an error like the following:  >> parpool('Processes', 4);    Parallel pool failed to start with the following error. For more detailed information, validate the profile 'Processes' in the Cluster Profile Manager.    Caused by:    Error using parallel.internal.pool.AbstractInteractiveClient>iThrowWithCause    Failed to initialize the interactive session.    Error using  parallel.internal.pool.AbstractInteractiveClient>iThrowIfBadParallelJobStatus    The interactive communicating job errored with the following message: An unexpected error occurred accessing a parallel pool. The underlying error was: Failed to bind to endpoint: tcps://tcpnodelay=localhost:27470/protocol/catapult    Cause: resolve: The requested name is valid, but no data of the requested type was found [system:11004].?
Workaround: In MATLAB R2023a, run these commands in the MATLAB editor before you start any parallel pools using the 'Processes' parallel environment:>> pctconfig('allserversocketsincluster', false);>> pctconfig('localpoolsbindtoall', true);These settings will not persist between MATLAB restarts.

Summary: Wrong toolstrip section and error message when using Zedboard model with?Embedded Coder??Support Package for Xilinx??Zynq??Platform
Description: When you use?Embedded Coder??Support Package for Xilinx??Zynq??Platform with Zedboard, if you click the "Configure, Monitor & Tune" button, you get this error message: FPGA or processor model not detected. You must launch SoC Builder from the top model. If the error persists, see "Build Error for FPGA or Processor Model not Detected". The Simulink? toolstrip also changes to show a "Configure, Build, and Deploy" button for users without SoC Blockset?. The Simulink toolstrip should remain unchanged and show a single click build instead of the "Configure, Build, and Deploy" app-based build.

Summary: Training and inference on NVIDIA Ampere and Ada Lovelace GPUs might error
Description: Training or inference (using?predict , classify , or activations ) ?with a convolutional dlnetwork  or DAGNetwork ? might result in an error. The error might occur when networks with particular input sizes perform convolution operations or when a convolution layer is followed by a ReLU layer. This bug has been observed on A5000, A30, RTX 3070, and RTX 4090 GPUs. The?error encountered is: cuDNN failed to return a valid plan for cudnnBackendExecute for convolution.

Summary: HDL Coder does not generate reusable code for atomic masked subsystems when SubsystemReuse is set to Atomic and Virtual
Description: You can generate reusable code for atomic masked subsystems by setting MaskParameterAsGeneric  to on . When you set Code Reuse  (SubsystemReuse ) to Atomic and Virtual ,?the code reuse and creation of generics is broken. Each masked subsystem might generate its own HDL file.

Summary: SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP?option for an S-Function block is not honored when another such S-Function block is placed inside a virtual subsystem at the same level of model hierarchy
Description: When you specify the SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP  option for an S-Function block, the block is supposed to be placed before other blocks in the sorted list. However, if you specify the same option for another S-Function block and place it inside a virtual subsystem at the same level of model hierarchy as the S-Function block outside the subsystem, the SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP  option for the S-Function block outside the subsystem is not honored.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Updating model containing Stateflow charts and custom code might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when updating a Simulink model where all of the following conditions are true: The model has multiple Stateflow charts The model has custom code specified in its?Simulation Target  configuration parameters Simulation Target ?> ?Advanced parameters >? Import custom code ?is selected  The generated access violation stack trace refers to "CG_Obj::propagateTags ".
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Property Inspector in App Designer is blank in Japanese locale
Description: After dragging a component from the Component Library onto the App Designer canvas, the Property Inspector is blank.
Workaround: Set the Help language to English. On the Home tab, in the Environment section, click?Preferences?and select MATLAB > Help > Language?> English?and restart MATLAB. Then, to view the documentation in Japanese, use the translated online documentation.

Summary: Incorrect violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A20-8-6
Description: Polyspace incorrectly raises a violation AUTOSAR-C++14 A20-8-6 when the copy  constructor of a smart pointer is used.

Summary: Code generation might fail with 'Invariant Transpose Typing?violated by object' assertion
Description: Code generation for MATLAB code that does not contain a matrix transpose expression might result in a crash with the following message: Invariant Transpose Typing violated by object : transpose output type has dimension of wrong size  The intermediate optimizations might produce a matrix transpose expression and an internal check incorrectly flags the dimensions of the expression.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Starting a parpool using the 'Processes' parallel environment fails with more than 100 workers
Description: Whenyou start a parallel pool using the 'Processes' parallel environment with morethan 100 workers, pool creation fails with a message that refers to "Failedto bind to endpoint ". ?Forexample, this?parpool?command fails?with an error like the following: >>parpool('Processes',101);  Parallel pool failed to start with the following error. For more detailed information, validate the profile 'Processes' in the Cluster Profile Manager.  Caused by:  Error using parallel.internal.pool.AbstractInteractiveClient>iThrowWithCause  Failed to initialize the interactive session.  Error using parallel.internal.pool.AbstractInteractiveClient>iThrowIfBadParallelJobStatus  The interactive communicating job errored with the following message: An unexpected error occurred accessing a parallel pool. The underlying error was: Failed to bind to endpoint: tcp://tcpnodelay=localhost:27470/protocol/catapult  Cause: protectedInit():: No ports to bind!  Errors encountered:  Address already in use?
Workaround: Increasethe port range of the workers.1.In MATLAB R2023a, open the?taskStartup.m?file of your local machine in the MATLAB editor.>>open taskStartup.mAlternatively,you can find?taskStartup.m?file of your local machine at this location:Windows:?<matlabroot>\toolbox\parallel\user\taskStartup.m?Linux:<matlabroot>/toolbox/parallel/user/taskStartup.m?2.Add this command to your?taskStartup.m?file?pctconfig('portrange',[2737028370])

Summary: Invoking a handle to the colon operator produces an unexpected result
Description: Invoking a function handle to the colon operator produces an unexpected result. For example, calling colon(1,2,10) ?results in [1 3 5 7 9] . However, invoking a handle to colon  produces a different value: >> fh = @colon;>> fh(1,2,10)ans =     2     1    10

Summary: 3-D View does not update Feed/Via/Load and Dielectric Layers
Description: In the PCB Antenna Designer App, 3-D view of a PCB antenna model retains the previously rendered feed, via, load and dielectric layers even after deleting them from the tree/2-D Canvas.
Workaround: Save the PCB antenna model and open the saved model in a new app instance then view the model. This correctly shows the updated model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when a block with initial conditions is connected to an Assignment block having an?external index source
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect initialization code for a model containing a block with initial conditions when all  of these conditions are true:?? The block with initial conditions is connected to the?Input Data Port? (U ) of an Assignment block. The Assignment block is configured to have an external index port by setting the?Index Option? parameter? to?Index vector (port) ?or?Starting index (port) . The external index port is connected to an external indices source.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: reshape function with dlarray input
Description: Code generated for the?reshape ?function that is passed a?dlarray ?input might return incorrect answer if?all ?these conditions are true: The size argument of reshape  is a row vector. The number of elements of this row vector is equal to the number of dimensions of the input.? One or more elements of this row vector?are identical to the sizes of certain dimensions of the dlarray  input.  For example, this code might produce incorrect answer in code generation: dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = reshape(dlx, [1 3 20]);
Workaround: Call the extractdata function on the input before calling the reshape function.dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = dlarray(reshape(extractdata(dlx), [1 3 20])); Alternatively, pass the size arguments as a comma separated list to the?reshape?function.dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = reshape(dlx, 1, 3, 20);
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Figure toolbar missing for figures that are shown in Simulation Manager
Description: The figure toolbar that appears at the top-right corner on mouse hover, is missing for figures that are shown in the Simulation Manager window.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder generates incorrect code for certain atomic operations
Description: Under certain conditions, GPU Coder might generate incorrect code when atomic operations (e.g. gpucoder.atomicAdd ) are used in the application. This issue might occur if the following conditions are met: The user code contains a for-loop that performs an atomic operation on some variable t   The loop is allowed to execute on GPU as a result of the coder.gpu.kernelfun  pragma being specified. Either of the following are true: t  is read or written inside the loop by an operation that is not an atomic operation.  t  is modified inside the loop by two different kinds of atomic operations. (For instance,  t  is first modified by a call to  gpucoder.atomicAdd  followed by a call to  gpucoder.atomicMax.)     Note that this issue can be triggered even if the loop is not tagged with the coder.gpu.kernel  pragma. Below is an example that triggers this issue: function t = entryPoint(n)??? coder.gpu.kernelfun;??? t = 0;??? for i = 1:n??????? t = 1;??????? t = gpucoder.atomicAdd(t, 1);??? endend
