Summary: Calling visastatus on a VISA-USB device causes Query Unterminated?device error
Description: When connected to a USB device using the visadev  interface, calling the visastatus  function on it incorrectly reads the status multiple times. This causes the device to issue a Query Unterminated  error, which results in a MATLAB error. The visastatus  function behaves as expected for all other?visadev  interfaces (TCP/IP, GPIB, Serial, VXI, and PXI) by reading the status a single time.

Summary: Error thrown during code generation when a Simulink Function or Function Caller has a custom function prototype defined or called in a Stateflow chart.
Description: When the function prototype for a Simulink Function or Function Caller is customized for a function that is defined or called from a Stateflow chart, an error is thrown during code generation. Prior to R2022a, customizing the function prototype for Simulink Functions and Function Callers was not supported. In R2022a and later, you can customize the function prototype from the context menu of the Simulink Function or Function Caller block by selecting?C/C++ Code > Configure C/C++ Function Interface .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer and Model Builder apps do not warn when opening a project saved in a later release
Description: SimBiology does not support forward compatibility of its project format. The apps might try to load projects that were saved in later versions without displaying any warnings or errors, but the integrity of the project cannot be guaranteed and the project data structures might become inconsistent with any particular version.? You should not attempt to load projects saved in later versions of SimBiology Model Builder  and SimBiology Model Analyzer .
Workaround: You can save and export models to MAT-files and use them in any release from R2019b and later.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Description: MATLAB might crash when running classify  on Linux. If you launch MATLAB from a shell, the shell shows the following message: Could not load library libcudnn_cnn_infer. so. 8.  Error: libnvrtc. so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory? Summary: 5G reference applications that perform SIB1 recovery report incorrect frequency offset estimates
Description: After the first SSB detected in a search operation, the  nrhdlSIB1Recovery Simulink(R) model reports incorrect frequency offsets for subsequent SSBs. This error occurs because the demodulation mode frequency tracking loop is incorrectly enabled in search mode and so each detection is performed relative to the previous estimate.?  The nrhdlSIB1Recovery model is used in the?NR HDL SIB1 Recovery ?and?NR HDL SIB1 Recovery FR2 ?examples. The same issue exists in the soc_nrhdlSIB1Recovery_fpga model used in the?5G NR SIB1 Recovery for FR1 and FR2 Using Xilinx RFSoC Device ?example.

Summary: On deploying Database Toolbox code as a part of multiple CTFs, on MATLAB Production Server, the Database Toolbox code in the second CTF archive does not run correctly
Description: On deploying Database Toolbox code as a part of multiple CTFs, on MATLAB Production Server, the Database Toolbox code in the second CTF archive does not run correctly. This is because, MATLAB Production Server operates in a Multi MVM-Sigle MCOS environment whereas Database Toolbox code is not compatible on this environment. This leads to the behavior described in the problem summary.

Summary: Requirements for model coverage filtering missing from the?Simulink Coverage Tool Operational Requirements?artifact
Description: The Simulink Coverage Tool Operational Requirements   artifact (mathworks_slcov_tor ) does not provide requirements for the model coverage filtering functionality that is supported by the IEC Certification Kit. The missing requirements include:  Simulink Coverage Coverage shall provide the ability to implement a coverage filter to exclude the coverage for: CVF_BLK_EXC - a block by its path CVF_BTYPE_EXC - all instances of a block by its block type CVF_SUB_EXC - a subsystem and all its descendants CVF_LIB_EXC - all instances of a library block CVF_MASK_EXC - all instances of a block with a given mask type CVF_STATE_EXC - a state with all descendants in a State Chart CVF_TRNS_EXC - a transition in a State Chart   Simulink Coverage shall provide the ability to implement a coverage filter to justify all missing coverage objectives for: CVF_BLK_JFY - a block by its path CVF_BTYPE_JFY - all instances of a block by its block type CVF_SUB_JFY - a subsystem and all its descendants CVF_LIB_JFY - all instances of a library block CVF_TRNS_JFY - a transition in a State Chart   Simulink Coverage shall provide the ability to implement a coverage filter to justify specific coverage objective outcomes for: CVF_BLK_OJFY - a block by its path CVF_SUB_OJFY - a conditionally executed subsystem CVF_CHART_OJFY - a transition in a State Chart

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected simulation output when loading duplicate variables from a single data source using loadVariablesFromExternalSource
Description: When an external data source contains multiple variables with the same name, you cannot predict the order in which the function loadVariablesFromExternalSource  loads the variables into a Simulink.SimulationInput  object, resulting in unexpected simulation results.This issue can occur, for example, when the external file is a data dictionary. If the data dictionary and one or more of its referenced dictionaries each contain a definition of variable x , you can cannot predict which definition of x  that the function loadVariablesFromExternalSource  loads into the Simulink.SimulationInput  object.

Summary: signedDistanceMap?object calculates distances and gradients in grid coordinates instead of scaling with resolution
Description: The signedDistanceMap ? object's distance  and gradient  functions compute distance and gradient values for a grid coordinate frame, regardless of the specified frame ?argument value. This may result in silent wrong answers (SWA) when you specify locations in a local or world coordinate frame because the functions calculate the distance and gradient values as if the locations are in a grid coordinate frame.
Workaround: When using?distance or gradient?functions without specifying a frame (default frame should be "world") or by?specifying the frame as "local", use the following code as an example of how to get the correct distance and gradient values:% Calculate distances and gradients for random locations in a signed distance map for both local and world coordinate framesmap = signedDistanceMap(eye(100),Resolution=10);locations = rand(10,2);gridDist1 = distance(map,locations);gridDist2 = distance(map,locations,"local");gridGrad1 = gradient(map,locations);gridGrad2 = gradient(map,locations,"local")% Convert distances and gradients to the correct Cartesian scale by dividing the distance and gradient values by the resolution of the mapcartDist1 = gridDist1/map.Resolution;cartDist2 = gridDist2/map.Resolution;cartGrad1 = gridGrad1/map.Resolution;cartGrad2 = gridGrad2/map.Resolution;Note that results from?distance?and gradient?do not need to be corrected if you are specifying locations in a grid coordinate frame and the frame?argument is?"grid".

Summary: Setting UseFloatingPoint to on through the MATLAB command line can cause a corruption of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Description: When you use the?hdlset_param  function to? set the UseFloatingPoint? property to  on  for a model , the Configuration Parameters dialog box corrupts by preventing you from setting any configuration parameters and displays this error message: An unexpected error occurred while creating Model Configuration Parameters dialog box.? This might be caused by functions in your MATLAB path that shadow built-in MATLAB functions.?  ? ? Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.?  ? ? file:<matlabroot>/toolbox/shared/configset/+configset/+internal/+customwidget/HDLFloatingPointVendorLibraryEnums.p?  ? ? name: HDLFloatingPointVendorLibraryEnums?  ? ? line: 41?  These are the steps to reproduce the issue:? 1. Open a Simullink model with UseFloatingPoint?  parameter set to off . 2. Run this command:?hdlset_param(gcs, 'UseFloatingPoint', 'on')?  3. Open the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Note that this corruption of the Configuration Parameters dialog box only happens when you set? UseFloatingPoint  through the command line, not through the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: Workaround 1:In addition to using the command hdlset_param(gcs, 'UseFloatingPoint', 'on'),?set the FloatingPointTargetConfiguration?parameter by using these commands:?fpconfig = hdlcoder.createFloatingPointTargetConfig(Name, Value)hdlset_param(gcs, 'FloatingPointTargetConfiguration', fpconfig)?hdlset_param(gcs, 'UseFloatingPoint', 'on')?Workaround 2:?Instead of using the function?hdlset_param?to enable floating-point support for the model, open the the Configuration Parameters dialog box and select the Use Floating Point checkbox in the?HDL Code Generation >?Floating Point pane. Floating point support is then enabled for the model.

Summary: Changing display name through Model Advisor Configuration Editor for custom configuration removes subchecks
Description: When you rename the display name of a Model Advisor check through the Model Advisor Configuration Editor,  the subchecks below it?disappear.  This behavior occurs for a custom configuration in the Model Advisor Configuration Editor.

Summary: SD card image creation in the Radio Setup Wizard cannot be completed
Description: The Radio Setup Wizard uses Windows Powershell commands to find and format the microSD card. Some Windows machines have Powershell ExecutionPolicy ? settings that restrict execution of Powershell scripts. The error message is: Unable to load screen wt.internal.hwsetup.screen.SelectDriveDetails: Failed to get list of physical disks.?
Workaround: This issue has been resolved in the latest hardware support package release. Please update to version 23.1.1 or later of the Wireless Testbench Support Package for NI USRP Radios.

Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR C++14 A2-7-2
Description: Polyspace incorrectly interprets some comments containing the character '#' as code, and raises a false positive violation of rule AUTOSAR C++14 A2-7-2. For example, Polyspace reports this comment as a violation even though no code is commented out: // Step #
Workaround: These workarounds are possible:Start your comment by using Doxygen-style syntax:?///, //*, or /**.Repeat a character multiple times in your comment: // ===== Step #

Summary: IP core generation with AXI4-Stream interface that has multiple clock rates generates?incorrect data rate in the HDL code.?
Description: When you generate the IP core with AXI4-stream interface that runs slower than the fastest clock rate, the clock divider logic wrongly computes rate transition between fast rate on the AXI4-Stream interfaces and the slow rate on the DUT. The computed rate transition is off by 1 ?clock cycle compared to the actual rates .? For example, the AXI4-Stream wrapper clock rate and user-stream clock rate is having ratio of?2:1 , whereas the ?clock divider logic computes rate transition of 3:1  in the generated HDL code instead of 2:1 .

Summary: Configure button appears dimmed when Third-party tool setting is LDRAcover or LDRA tool suite
Description: If you set the configuration parameter Third-party tool  to LDRAcover or LDRA tool suite  and the installation folder for LDRA software is not already specified, clicking the Configure  button does not open the Code Coverage Settings dialog box. The?Configure? button appears dimmed and you cannot specify the installation folder for the LDRA software.
Workaround: From the command line, specify the installation folder for the LDRA software :setpref('CodeCoverage_LDRA', 'Path', 'pathToInstallationFolder')?

Summary: Truth table transitions are flagged by checks "mathworks.jmaab.jc_0752" and "mathworks.jmaab.jc_0774" and cannot be fixed
Description: Check usage of parentheses in Stateflow transitions  (Check ID:? mathworks.jmaab.jc_0752)? flags Stateflow truth table transitions even though you can not add a new line before the condition and action statement. Check for comments in unconditional transitions  (Check ID:? mathworks.jmaab.jc_0774) ?flags unconditional transitions in Stateflow truth table transitions even though you can not add comments to truth tables.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: MATLAB graphics objects, such as axes, plots, and lines, do not render in?MATLAB Web App Server
Description: When you run a web app hosted using MATLAB Web App Server, any axes and all content within the axes will not appear. Additionally, any interactivity such as mouse or key callbacks on the axes will not be active.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Automatic parallelization of for loops containing saturating reduction operations on integer types
Description: This issue affects the generated code if all  these conditions are true:?? Your MATLAB code contains a for  loop that contains reduction operations on integer data? The configuration parameters Enable automatic parallelization? (EnableAutoParallelization ) and Optimize reductions  ( OptimizeReductions ) are enabled  The configuration parameter? Saturate on integer overflow ?(SaturateOnIntegerOverflow )?is enabled  In such situations, the code generator produces parallel?for  loops that perform arithmetic reduction operations on variables of saturating integer type. However, saturating integer reduction operations are not associative because the final result of several such operations depend on the order in which the threads execute. This might cause the concurrent code to produce numerically incorrect results when compared with the results produced by the sequential code.
Workaround: Do any one of the following:?Disable the configuration parameter?Optimize reductions?(OptimizeReductions). However, this action disables parallelization of for loops that contain any type of reduction operations - arithmetic, logical, min, max.If you can identify the loop that might cause this issue, you can disable parallelization of that specific loop by adding the coder.loop.parallelize('never') pragma immediately before that loop in your MATLAB code. For example,coder.loop.parallelize('never');for ii = 1:numel(arr1)    var = var + arr1(ii);end
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Description: When your input model contains Bus Element ports that are specified using certain data types at model reference, the generated model for Bus Element ports has an 'inherit' property. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder may generate incorrect code for For Each blocks which have silent vector conversion on their inputs
Description: In some cases, the For Each Subsystem may apply vector conversion to vector inputs to find non-singleton partition dimensions. The For Each Subsystem block may convert the vector before partitioning if these conditions are not met: If a For Each Subsystem is partitioning a row vector, then the Partition Dimension ?parameter must be set to 2 . If a For Each Subsystem is partitioning an unoriented or column vector, then the Partition Dimension  parameter?must be set to 1 .

Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 M8-5-2 on standard template library (STL) containers
Description: Polyspace raises false positive violations of AUTOSAR-C++14 M8-5-2 on containers that are part of the standard template library, such as? std::vector , std::array , std::list , std::map , etc. This rule applies to only arrays, structures, and union types.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Instrumentation overhead of PIL execution-time profiling not removed for target applications that use downward timers
Description: If the instrumentation overhead for execution-time profiling is estimated by a benchmark program at the start of a processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation, the computed overhead is null for target applications that use downward timers. Therefore, the reported execution times for the generated code includes the overhead associated with code instrumentation. For PIL target applications that use upward timers, the benchmark program calculates the code instrumentation overhead correctly.?
Workaround: You can use the target package to specify values for the code instrumentation overhead. For more information, see?Remove Instrumentation Overheads from Execution Time Measurements.

Summary: Default reference design parameter values for a Simulink model are shown as an empty cell array in MATLAB command window
Description: When you want to access the default reference design parameter values in the MATLAB command window or MATLAB script by using?hdlget_param  command , it displays an empty cell array. For example,? hdlget_param(<DUT_Name>, 'ReferenceDesignParameter')  ans =  ? 0ˇÁ0 empty cell array

Summary: Requirements for model coverage filtering missing from the Simulink Coverage Tool Operational Requirements artifact
Description: The Simulink Coverage Tool Operational Requirements  artifact (mathworks_slcov_tor ) does not provide requirements for the model coverage filtering functionality that is supported by the DO Qualification Kit. The missing requirements include: Simulink Coverage?Coverage shall provide the ability to implement a coverage filter to exclude the coverage for:  CVF_BLK_EXC - a block by its path CVF_BTYPE_EXC - all instances of a block by its block type CVF_SUB_EXC - a subsystem and all its descendants CVF_LIB_EXC - all instances of a library block CVF_MASK_EXC - all instances of a block with a given mask type CVF_STATE_EXC - a state with all descendants in a State Chart CVF_TRNS_EXC - a transition in a State Chart   Simulink Coverage shall provide the ability to implement a coverage filter to justify all missing coverage objectives for:  CVF_BLK_JFY - a block by its path CVF_BTYPE_JFY - all instances of a block by its block type CVF_SUB_JFY - a subsystem and all its descendants CVF_LIB_JFY - all instances of a library block CVF_TRNS_JFY - a transition in a State Chart   Simulink Coverage shall provide the ability to implement a coverage filter to justify specific coverage objective outcomes for:  CVF_BLK_OJFY - a block by its path CVF_SUB_OJFY - a conditionally executed subsystem CVF_CHART_OJFY - a transition in a State Chart

Description: When triggering job and task callback functions, MATLAB Job Schedulermight crash and become unresponsive. The crash is more likely to occur when these conditions apply: MATLAB Job Scheduler jobs and tasks use custom callback functions. MATLAB client is disconnected from the MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster. Default location: C:\MATLAB\R2023a\toolbox\parallel\bin\hs_err_pid772.mdmp  INFO   | jvm 1    | 2023/03/23 18:44:15 | #  INFO   | jvm 1    | 2023/03/23 18:44:15 | # An error report file with more information is saved as:  INFO   | jvm 1    | 2023/03/23 18:44:15 | # C:\MATLAB\R2023a\toolbox\parallel\bin\hs_err_pid772.log  The associated hs_err_pidNNN.log file shows a crash log that mightcontain these lines: Java frames: (J=compiled Java code, j=interpreted, Vv=VM code)  j? com.mathworks.toolbox.distcomp.spf.SPFCallback.nativeFire(J[B)V+0  j? com.mathworks.toolbox.distcomp.spf.SPFCallback.fire([B)V+6  j? com.mathworks.toolbox.distcomp.mjs.jobmanager.spf.MJSEventCallback.fire(Lnet/jini/id/Uuid;Lcom/mathworks/toolbox/distcomp/mjs/workunit/events/Event;)V+41  j? com.mathworks.toolbox.distcomp.mjs.workunit.events.ClientCallbackEventListener.eventOccurred(Lcom/mathworks/toolbox/distcomp/mjs/workunit/events/Event;)Z+22
Workaround: Perform a clean restart of the MATLAB Job Scheduler job manager process from the command line.Note: A clean restart deletes all information stored on disk from the previous job manager so that the job manager starts with no existing jobs or tasks.On the head node, run these commands:?Linux:cd <matlabroot>/toolbox/parallel/bin./mjs stop -clean./mjs start -clean./startjobmanager -clean??Windows:?cd <matlabroot>\toolbox\parallel\binmjs stop -cleanmjs start -clean?startjobmanager -clean<matlabroot> is the path to the root folder of your MATLAB installation.

Description: The code generator might not generate a sending service call to set an output value when?all ?of the following are true: The model contains a subsystem. That subsystem contains an Initialize, Reinitialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block. The Initialize, Reinitialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block is connected to a root-level outport. That root-level outport maps to a sender service.       For example, consider this generated code from a top model: void TopModelRootOutport_initialize(void)  {  ? (void) memset((void *)&TopModelRootOutport_DW, 0,  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? sizeof(DW_TopModelRootOutport_T));  ? TopModelRootOutport_instance1_Init();  }   Consider the initialization code of a subsystem in that top model:? void TopModelRootOutport_instance1_Init(void)  {  ? TopModelRootOutport_DW.Out = 1.2 *  ? ? get_TopModelRootOutport_initialize_In2();  }?   The generated code initializes?TopModelRootOutport_DW.Out ?in the subsystem? initialization function?TopModelRootOutport_instance1_Init , but the top model initialization function? Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A8-5-1 on data member initialized with NSDMI
Description: Polyspace raises a false positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A8-5-1 on classes that initialize nonstatic data-members during declaration. For example: class Bar{public:    explicit Bar(std::uint32_t& p): dm1(p), dm2(p)  // False positive violationprivate:    std::uint32_t& dm1;    std::uint32_t dm2;    std::mutex myMutex{};      // myMutex initialized with NSDMI};
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: kurtogram code generation errors for fixed-size input signals longer than 255 samples
Description: C/C++ code generation for the kurtogram  function throws this error when the input signal vector has a fixed size greater than 255: Size mismatch (size [256 x 1] ~= size [0 x 0]).The size to the left is the size of the left-hand side of the assignment.
Workaround: Instruct MATLAB Coder to disregard the size of the input signal during code generation. For example, instead of:>> kgram = kurtogram(x)use:>> kgram = kurtogram(coder.ignoreSize(x))

Summary: Updating model containing function-call subsystem with direct input to output connection via Merge loop crashes Simulink
Description: When all these conditions are met, Simulink crashes when updating the model. The model contains a function-call subsystem. The function-call subsystem has a direct connection between its input and output ports. The subsystem output corresponding to the outport port loops back to a subsystem input port via a Merge block.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might behave incorrectly for model using messages and default data mapping
Description: The generated code for a model might behave incorrectly when these conditions are true:?The model uses messages. The default mapping for internal data is to a package-based storage class.

Summary: Simulation 3D Camera block does not issue a warning when the resolution is set above 1920 by 1080 pixels
Description: Simulation 3D Camera block was developed and verified for the maximal resolution of 1920 by 1080 pixels. Simulation results can be invalid when the resolution is set above 1920 by 1080 pixels.

Summary: Properties of a dlnetwork?might be returned incorrectly
Description: Getting the Learnables  or State  property of a dlnetwork  returns a table with layer name, parameter name and the associated parameter value. In some cases, the layer and parameter names do not match the actual value of the parameter. Retrieving the parameter value by searching for the layer name returns the parameter value of a different layer.? This issue does not affect training or inference using a dlnetwork .

Summary: Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block outputs random number when input is zero
Description: When the input u ?to the Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block is zero, the block outputs a random number. The expected behavior is to saturate on overflow for division by zero.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Build process might fail if the generated code contains class properties whose names match with other class names
Description: Generated C++ code might fail to build if the name of a user-defined class property matches with the name of another class. For example, consider this generated C++ code:? typedef class {}conflict;class someclass{    int conflict;    conflict b[2]; };? The property name of class  someclass matches with another class name  conflict. If you try to build the generated code, the code generator issues this error: ?error: 'conflict' does not name a type |     conflict b[2]; |           ^~~~~~~~ Compiler returned: 1? C++ compilers do not allow field names to be the same as any other field type name. The build failure happens because the code generator does not rename the fields that are of a pointer or matrix type.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: A model containing a Complex to Real-Imag block and using a mixed floating-point configuration can lead to inaccurate simulation results.
Description: If you have a model that includes a Complex to Real-Imag block and you use a mixed floating-point configuration to map your design to the native floating-point (NFP) and a vendor-specific floating-point library, all blocks in the network that contain the Complex to Real-Imag block are converted to NFP implementation and generate HDL code accordingly. The generated HDL code is valid and functions as expected. However, during testbench generation, blocks in the network that are supported by the vendor floating-point library use latency from the vendor library rather than the NFP library, which can result in discrepancies between the expected and actual values when running an HDL simulation with a simulator such as ModelSim. This is an example of the simulation result where the actual value has a delay of 10ns compared to the expected value: ?# ** Error: Error in Out2_im: Expected C0D049F51D2824A4  Actual? 0000000000000000  ?#? ? Time: 120 ns? Iteration: 1? Instance: /subsystem_tb  ?# ** Error: Error in Out2_im: Expected 3FFB242F10A9D52E  Actual? C0D049F51D2824A4   ?#? ? Time: 130 ns? Iteration: 1? Instance: /subsystem_tb  ?# ** Error: Error in Out2_im: Expected C0D049F51D2824A4 Actual? 3FFB242F10A9D52E   ?#? ? Time: 140 ns? Iteration: 1? Instance: /subsystem_tb
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds to fix the simulation results:Use NFP configuration only and do not target a vendor-specific floating-point library.Move the Complex to Real-Imag block to a subsystem.

Summary: Commented Subsystem Reference block causes Simulink to crash when compiling model
Description: If a Simulink Subsystem Reference block is commented, Simulink might crash during model compilation during sample time post propagation. The stack trace is similar to: [0] 0x00007ffa950356ea slexec::sto::rate::RateSpec::rateType  [1] 0x00007ffa95034de3 slexec::sto::rate::RateSpec::isTriggered [2] 0x00007ffa95034dba slexec::sto::rate::Rate::isTriggered?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a referenced model with a feedback loop
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a referenced model. The top model contains a feedback loop where the output of the referenced model is directly fed into its input signal. The referenced model contains a function-call subsystem whose execution is controlled by a?Trigger port. The input port inside the referenced model is configured to latch the input value and prevent it from changing during the execution of the function-call subsystem by selecting the inport block parameter?Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs .?  If you generate code from a model that meets the above conditions, the code generator might eliminate temporary variables incorrectly. Incorrect elimination of local variables occurs when a function in the generated code has pointer arguments, and the caller of the function passes a memory address more than once as the pointer arguments. For example, if a top model calls a function defined in the referenced model as?botModel(&a, &b, &b) , where the memory address?&b ?is passed to the function?botModel ?twice, the generated code might be incorrect.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Order of the typedef statements in a header file might change when you regenerate code
Description: The order of the typedef ?statements in generated header files might change when you regenerate code while all ?of these conditions are met:  The model references a data type object. The data type object specifies a value for the HeaderFile  property. The data type object DataScope ?property has a value other than  Imported .   ?The changed typedef ?statement order does not affect the code functionality.

Summary: Unnecessary second initialization of internal "guard" variable
Description: The generated code for a given Simulink model may contain an internal "guard" variable when all of the following conditions are true:  The same inputs go into multiple conditional blocks(for instance if-else ladders, switch blocks, relational blocks)which branch out These branches then merge into another conditional block   The "guard" variable is initialized on declaration, and then again later, with no intervening reads or writes of the variable, like so: boolean guard1 = false;? ...? guard1 = false;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: issimplified method returns true for?polyshape objects created with?Simplify?option set to false
Description: Suppose that you create a?polyshape  object using a method that accepts the Simplify ?name-value argument, which you set to?false . If you then call the issimplified  method on this polyshape  object, the method returns true .

Summary: 'X does not support locale' warning on Linux
Description: When starting MATLAB on a Linux system, the Command Window displays this warning: Warning: X does not support locale  The warning has no functional impact, so you can ignore it.

Summary: Loop induction variables of a For-loop might be incorrectly updated if the?for-loop does not execute
Description: Incorrect code might generate for a MATLAB function that meets all  of these conditions:? The MATLAB function contains a for -loop with induction variables. The upper and lower bounds of the induction variables are specified in the loop. There is a possibility of the loop not getting executed at run-time.  Consider the following MATLAB code: function [k,m] = r(s,e)    k = int32(0);    m = int32(zeros(1,2048));    for idx = s : e        k = k + 1;        m(k) = idx;    endend? MATLAB Coder generates the following code snippet:?? for (idx = s; idx <= e; idx++) {    m[idx - s] = idx;  }  *k = (e - s) + 1; Notice that if the for -loop does not execute at run-time, the variable k  will hold an incorrect answer.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: After running clear classes or clear functions certain features no longer open
Description: After running the clear classes  or clear functions  commands, certain windows do not open. These features include App Designer , UIFigure , Add-On Explorer , Simulink Start Page , Simulink Data Explorer  and others. A window for the feature briefly appears and then disappears.
Workaround: Run this command at MATLAB startup or add to the startup.m file:setenv('MW_CEF_ENABLE_BINARY_TRANSPORT','0');

Summary: Error during code generation when model contains multiple lookup tables with table values from an input port
Description: An error occurs during code generation of an AUTOSAR component model that contains multiple Interpolation using Prelookup or Direct Lookup Table blocks when one of the following conditions are true: The lookup table values come from an input port. The lookup table values come from?a Constant block whose values are?defined by a?Simulink.Parameter  object.   If either of these conditions are true, the following error is produced during code generation. The call to autosar_make_rtw_hook, during the after_tlc hook generated the following error: Not enough input arguments.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Image Resizer HDL code returns incorrect output pixel values when performing a horizontal-only resize
Description: This issue applies to the Image Resizer block and the visionhdl.ImageResizer System object. The simulation behaviour of the Image Resizer block and the visionhdl.ImageResizer System object is correct. The generated HDL code may return incorrect output pixel values when InputSize height == OutputSize height, such as a resize from [48, 64] to [48, 48].  The presence and severity of pixel output errors depends on the vertical contrast in the input data, the fixed-point rounding method, and the horizontal resize scale factor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel with row majority and non-commutative operation
Description: Code generated for gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel ?? might produce incorrect results if both  these conditions are satisfied:  The function that calls   gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel uses row-major layout.   The function handle passed to   gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel is non-commutative.    For example, the issue affects the generated GPU code for this MATLAB function: function C = entryPoint(A, B)% A and B are numeric arrayscoder.rowMajor;func = @(x,y) x-y;C = gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel(func, A, B);end

Summary: MATLAB might crash during HDL code generation when you enable?BalanceClockRateOutputPorts
Description: When you enable BalanceClockRateOutputPorts , a crash might occur during HDL code generation.  Abnormal termination:  Access violation  pir::BaseRatePartitionerImpl::balanceClockRateOutputs at B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\BaseRatePartitioner.cpp:2007
Workaround: Set?BalanceClockRateOutputPorts to off.>> hdlset_param(bdroot, 'BalanceClockRateOutputPorts', 'off')

Summary: Generated code for a model with Concurrent Tasks turned on might produce a resource deadlock error
Description: A model produces a run-time error during execution of the generated code if the following conditions are true: Model has ConcurrentTasks  parameter turned on Model contains Stateflow charts accessing global data store memories  In this scenario, the generated code might contain multiple consecutive data protection lock/unlock code for each read and write accessing the same global data store memory.
Workaround: Do not read and write to the global data store memory in the same statement of a Stateflow Chart.

Summary: datcomimport?function for cases with HORIZONTAL STABILIZER COEFFICIENTS swap CL and CD?values
Description: When calculating untrimmed and trimmed coefficients for a movable horizontalstabilizer,?the?datcompimport? function swaps the values for the struct variables cd_tailutrim  ?and cl_tailutrim  ?and the values for cd_tailtrim  ?and cl_tailtrim  .
Workaround: After running datcomimport for an affected case, swap the values for variables cd_tailutrim?and cl_tailutrim?and variables cd_tailtrim?and cl_tailtrim.

Summary: The movmedian method of fillmissing2 rounds negative integers incorrectly
Description: When MATLAB calculates the median of integer-type data and the median lies between two integer values, MATLAB rounds away from zero.  >>M = median(int32([1 2]))  M =    int32       2    >>M = median(int32([-1 -2]))  M =    int32       -2    ?However, the movmedian  method of fillmissing2  incorrectly rounds negative integers towards zero. >>F = fillmissing2(int32([-1 0 -2]),"movmedian",3,MissingLocations=[false true false]) F =      1ˇÁ3 int32?row vector        -1   -1   -2
Workaround: Avoid this issue by casting integer-type data to double before calling fillmissing2, then casting the result to the original type.>>A =? int32([-1 0 -2]);>>F = fillmissing2(double(A),"movmedian",3,MissingLocations=[false true false]);>>F = int32(F)F=  1ˇÁ3 int32?row vector   -1   -2   -2

Summary: Minimum or Maximum block with Index output can cause assertion to be hit during HDL code generation
Description: When generating HDL code for a Minimum or Maximum block which has the Index output enabled, this internal assertion can be thrown: Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation. Contact support@mathworks.com with reproduction steps. Assertion Failed With Debug Message: matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\CompTransformer.cpp:149:(ipa->getNumPorts() == 2) && (opa->getNumPorts() == (singleOut ? 1 : 2))  This assertion might be thrown when native floating point is enabled for the model. ?
Workaround: Either disable native floating point for the model or remove the use of the Index output on the Minimum or Maximum block.

Summary: Generate HDL code for MATLAB Function block that contains the function?reinterpretcast might have simulation mismatch
Description: HDL code generated from a MATLAB Function block that contains the function ?reinterpretcast  might have unnecessary saturation logic that results in simulation mismatch. For example, this MATLAB code might show a mismatch in simulation results with the generated HDL code : out = reinterpretcast((fi(input,16,1),numerictype(1,16,15));
Workaround: Wrap the casting value using the?fi function and set the?OverflowAction?property to Wrap by using this command:fi(input, 'OverflowAction', 'Wrap')
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: External mode simulation with C++ target application fails
Description: An external mode simulation might fail if the model has this combination of configuration parameter settings: Language  ? is  C++  Use dynamic memory allocation for model initialization  check box is selected
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: MATLAB Support Package for Arduino Hardware fails for Bluetooth connections
Description: Test Connection for MATLAB Support Package for Arduino Hardware fails for Nano 33 BLE, Nano 33 BLE Sense, Nano 33 IoT, and MKR1010 boards when the connection type is Bluetooth, and throws the following error:  Test connection failed. Go back to check your settings. For more information, see Arduino Hardware Troubleshooting.
Workaround: For a workaround to this issue, contact MathWorks Technical Support at https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html.

Summary: Parallel tasks that use mpiexec might fail on Linux clusters without ˇ°libunwindˇ± installed
Description: When you use MATLAB Parallel Server? integrated to a Linux cluster that uses a third-party scheduler like SLURM, and the cluster machines do not have ˇ°libunwindˇ± installed, parallel tasks that use?mpiexec ?might fail to run with a message that refers to "libunwind.so.8 ".? For example, cluster validation might fail with an error like the following: /network/installs/matlab/r2023a/bin/glnxa64/mpiexec: error while loading shared libraries: libunwind.so.8: Cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
Workaround: Install "libunwind" on the cluster machines. For example, on systems that use apt-get, use this command:sudo apt-get install -y libunwind-dev?

Summary: Configuring memory sections in the code mappings for model parameter arguments has no impact on generated code
Description: In the Data Defaults  tab of the Code Mappings editor, when you configure the Memory Section  property of the storage class for model parameter arguments, there is no effect on the generated code.
Workaround: To configure memory sections for model parameter arguments, define a storage class and apply the memory section to the storage class. To use?const?or?volatile?qualifiers, configure the properties of the storage class.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: lsqnonlin and lsqcurvefit error when using parallel computation and a lower bound equals an upper bound
Description: lsqnonlin  and lsqcurvefit  throw an internal error when all of the following apply: Option UseParallel  is true . One or more lower bounds equal the corresponding upper bounds (when?any(lb == ub) ). Option Algorithm  is?trust-region-reflective  (the default).
Workaround: The error can be avoided by setting the Algorithm option to levenberg-marquardt:% Set optionsoptions = optimoptions('lsqnonlin', 'Algorithm', 'levenberg-marquardt', 'UseParallel', true);% Call solver[x, residual, resnorm] = lsqnonlin(objfun, x0, lb, ub, options);Alternatively, the variable dimensions with equal bounds can be removed from the problem before calling lsqnonlin or lsqcurvefit. This workaround is more involved and requires modifying the objective function as well.% Logical indices for variables with equal bounds, also referred to as fixed variablesfixedVariableIndex = lb == ub;% Remove fixed variablesx0 = x0(~fixedVariableIndex);Use the logical indices to remove and re-insert the fixed variables in the objective function, as shown in this code.% Make a handle to the objective function and snap in the fixed variable valuesfixedValues = lb(fixedVariableIndex);objfun = @(x) myfun(x, fixedValues, fixedVariableIndex);function F = myfun(x, fixedValues, fixedVariableIndex)% Re-insert fixed variable values in the "x" vectorcompleteX =  zeros(size(fixedVariableIndex));completeX(fixedVariableIndex) = fixedValues;completeX(~fixedVariableIndex) = x;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Dataflow subsystems using Automatic frame size calculation and directly connected to Atomic subsystems might produce incorrect results
Description: Dataflow subsystems might produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are met: ?Automatic frame size calculation is enabled for the Dataflow subsystem. The Dataflow subsystem is directly connected to the output of an Atomic subsystem. The frame size at the input to the Dataflow subsystem does not change (that is, the input signal is not buffered).  Under these conditions the block sorted execution might change and the Dataflow subsystem might produce incorrect results.
Workaround: Either disable Automatic frame size calculation or insert a non-virtual block, such as a Gain block, between the Dataflow subsystem and the Atomic subsystem.

Summary: MATLAB crashes after undo operation in MATLAB Function Block
Description: If you perform the following steps in a model that contains a MATLAB Function block with a non-tunable parameter variable, MATLAB crashes:? update or run the model delete the MATLAB Function block update the model undo the MATLAB Function block deletion update the model

Summary: Incorrect execution of timed events in Stateflow charts that are inside function-call subsystems with multiple initiators having different sample times
Description: Stateflow absolute time operators such as at , before , every , or after  may incorrectly evaluate to true when all the following conditions are met:? Stateflow chart is inside a function-call subsystem that has multiple initiators Function-call initiators have different sample times, causing the function-call subsystem to run at the union of those sample times
Workaround: Set the same sample time on all initiators of the parent function-call subsystem.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Generated HDL code for the Reciprocal block may have one bit simulation mismatch when using?ShiftAdd?architecture and custom output types.?
Description: HDL code generated for Reciprocal block or Math Function Reciprocal block?might result in a simulation mismatch of one bit when you set the following conditions for the blocks: Architecture  is set to ShiftAdd  Output data type  is selected other than? Inherit: Inherit via internal rule  Max(Input word length, Output?word length)  is equal to  Sum ( Input fractional length, Output fractional length, 1)
Workaround: Increase the Input or Output word length by one bit so that it meets following criteria:?? Max(Input word length, Output?word length)?must be one bit greater than?Sum(Input fractional length, Output fractional length, 1)And use Data Type Conversion block after the Reciprocal block or Math Function Reciprocal block to reduce the word length by one bit.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Static or global variables in custom code are not reset at the beginning of the simulation
Description: When custom code, containing global variables or functions using global and static variables, are accessed by C Caller blocks, C Function blocks, or Stateflow charts, the variables are not reset at the beginning of the simulation. As a result, in successive simulations the global and static variables still hold the values held during the end of the previous simulation.
Workaround: Use the following command at the end of a simulation to reset the custom code variables:>> clear mex;

Summary: AutoSAR access function calls might be generated for zero initialization of output ports even when Remove zero initialization for root I/O is selected
Description: The code generator might generate AutoSAR access function calls for zero initialization of output ports when the Remove zero initialization for root I/O  configuration parameter?is selected. For example, consider this generated code: void model11_Init(void)  {  ??(void)Rte_Write_out1_out1(model1.out1);  }   The generated code initializes?model1.out1  despite the Remove zero initialization for root I/O ?configuration parameter .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Subscripted assignment from constant floating-point value to fixed-point type can generate type mismatch error
Description: During HDL code generation of a DUT that contains a Stateflow chart, when a constant value of a floating-point type is implicitly casted to a fixed-point type via assignment, a type mismatch error can occur.  For example, if x is a constant of type double and y has a fixed-point type, this line of code can cause a type mismatch error: ? ? y(:) = x;?  This is the type mismatch error that occurs. ? A cast between fixpt and floating point type is not supported.
Workaround: To fix the error, cast the constant floating-point value explicitly to the intended fixed-point type:??? ? y(:) = cast(x, 'like', y);??

Summary: Sine Wave block with discrete sample time inside If Action Subsystem or Switch Case Action Subsystem block might output wrong answer?in normal simulation mode
Description: The output value of a Sine Wave block with its?Sine type ?set to Time based  and satisfying? all of the following conditions might not match the absolute time in normal simulation mode: The Sine Wave block is inside an If Action Subsystem or Switch Case Action Subsystem block. The action subsystem is nested inside another conditionally executed subsystem. The Sine Wave block has discrete?sample time. The action subsystem is not configured to reset when the subsystem reenables. The action subsystem might be disabled because its parent subsystem is disabled, although the action signals remain the same.  This issue only affects simulation in normal mode. Generated code is correct.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code produces incorrect numerical results for round with MinGW on Windows
Description: MATLAB Coder and Simulink Coder generate code that produces incorrect numerical results for round  operations on floating point numbers within eps  distance of 0.5 or -0.5. Other numeric values are not affected. The incorrect code generation occurs when all of these conditions are met: You generate code for a model or function that uses round , such as the MATLAB function round  or the corresponding Simulink Rounding Function   block The TargetLangStandard  configuration parameter is set to C++11 or C99. The C++ function std::round  or C function round ,? or any variant of those functions, is present in the generated code. The MinGW toolchain is used to compile the generated code.   The issue is due to a known MinGW bug that affects the MinGW implementation of the C standard library. For more information, see https://sourceforge.net/p/mingw-w64/bugs/573/.

Summary: Y-Junction(IL, TL) blocks calculate loss coefficients incorrectly for IdelˇŻchik correlation
Description: When the Loss coefficient model  parameter is set to IdelˇŻchikcorrelation , the loss coefficients are off by a factor equal to the square of the ratio ofthe branch and common channel flow velocities.

Summary: isShared == aFcn->isShared()?assertion when generating code
Description: The code generator might throw an error if you generate code from a Simulink model that meets all of the following conditions: The model?contains MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks that have subfunctions The subfunctions are not inlined. The model is configured to place its utility code in a shared location, or you generate model reference code from the model (for example, if you call slbuild(myModel,'ModelReferenceCoderTarget') ?to generate the code)  The error resembles the following: Code generation assertion 'isShared == aFcn->isShared()' failed?

Summary: Functions specklefilt and imregicp from Medical Imaging Toolbox error when used in deployed applications.
Description: Functions specklefilt  and imregicp  throw an error when used in deployed applications (.exe).  For example, the specklefilt  function, when used in a deployed application, throws the following error: Unable to resolve the name 'medical.internal.builtins.specklefilt_halide'. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation and HDL code generation mismatch for hybrid models in Simscape hardware-in-the-loop workflow?
Description: Simulation of hybrid designs containing Simulink and Simscape models with feedback loop sometimes might result in mismatch between generated HDL code and HDL compatible Simulink model output.

Summary: False positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 rule 20.9, AUTOSAR CPP14 rule M16-0-7, and MISRA C++:2008 rule 16-0-7
Description: Polyspace raises false positive violations of? MISRA-C:2012 rule 20.9, AUTOSAR CPP14 rule M16-0-7, and MISRA C++:2008 rule 16-0-7 when scanning the arguments of a function-like macro. Consider this code: #define YO XX                   #define LO YY                   #define XXXXYYYY 1              #define ADD(a,b) a##b           #define ADD2(a,b) ADD(a##a,b##b)#define ADD3(a,b) ADD2(a,b)     #if ADD3(YO,LO)   //False positive              #endif Polyspace incorrectly raised a violation on the #if  statement even though the token XXXXYYYY  is defined.

Summary: A saved .slx model file does not show any information for fields such as author, release, and category in Windows property handler Details panel
Description: When you save a .slx  model and then open it with the Windows property handler, all fields such as author, release, and category are empty, even if you have specified them in MATLAB. Also, you cannot change or add values for those fields in Windows property handler.

Summary: Generating PDF report containing nonprinting control characters in document heading or caption crashes MATLAB
Description: If you generate a PDF report that contains nonprinting control characters in a document heading, the following error occurs and MATLAB crashes. Unsupported control characters include characters with ASCII codes between 1 and 31, except horizontal tab (ASCII code 9), line feed (ASCII code 10), and carriage return (ASCII code 13). Error using mlreportgen.internal.fop.FOPObject/executeCannot convert "C:/workspace/root.fo" to "C:/workspace/test.pdf". Reason: Invalid Unicode character: U+001B? ? If the nonprinting character appears in a table of contents or list of captioned report elements, such as figure captions or table titles, MATLAB crashes.? For example, the following code adds the escape character to a document. MATLAB crashes when the document is closed. d = mlreportgen.dom.Document("output");append(d,mlreportgen.dom.TOC);append(d,mlreportgen.dom.Heading1(char(27))); close(d);
Workaround: Remove unsupported characters from the input string before adding the string to a DOM component or document. For example,inputChar = ['line feed: ',char(10),'escape character: ',char(27)];supportedChars = [9, 10, 13]; % Horizontal tab, line feed, and carriage return are supportedtoRemove = ~ismember(inputChar,supportedChars) & (inputChar < 32);inputChar(toRemove) = []
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong code generated when?Simulink.Signal object is attached to string signal
Description: Simulink.Signal  does not support string ?data type. However, Simulink fails to return an error if a Simulink.Signal  object with DataType  set to?auto ?is attached to a string signal in a model. Instead, the generated code produces wrong answers.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: C++ compilation error due to assignment operations to this pointer
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate assignment operations to this  pointer when generating C++ code from MATLAB code that satisfies all  of these conditions: The MATLAB code contains a handle class. The handle class has a non-static class method. The class method contains an arguments block  . The first argument declaration in the?arguments?block  ?includes a specification of the size of the argument.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef MyClass < handle  ??? ...   ??? methods   ??? ...   ??????? function Foo(obj)  ? ????????? arguments   ? ? ? ????????? obj (1, 1) {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")}  ? ? ? ????? end   ? ????????? ...   ??????? end   ??? end   end   MATLAB Coder generates this C++ code for the above MATLAB code:   void MyClass::Foo()  {  ??? this = handle_init();  ??? ...  }   In the generated code,? the first argument of the non-static class method in the MATLAB code is represented as?this  pointer, ?and an assignment operation happens to the pointer.?  Assignment to this  pointer ?is not allowed in C++. MSVC gives the following error message: error C2106: '=': left operand must be l-value  GCC gives the following error message: error: lvalue required as left operand of assignment   Clang gives the following error message:  error: expression is not assignable
Workaround: Remove the size specification from argument declarations in the arguments block.? In the MATLAB code example given above, replacing the code line obj (1, 1) {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")}?with?obj {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")} resolves the error.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?predict?function might produce incorrect results if 'CU' inputs are passed to dlnetwork
Description: Code generated for the dlnework ?object function?predict  might produce incorrect results if all  of these conditions are true:? Target library is 'cudnn '. The dlnetwork  does not have any input layer. For example, it has been initialized with example inputs. The data format of the?dlarray  you pass to predict ?is 'CU '.  Here is an example workflow that produces an incorrect answers: % Create example entry point function 'mpredict'function out = mpredict(matFile, in) %#codegennet = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork(matFile);out = predict(net, in);end% Define dlnetwork objectexampleInput = dlarray(ones(5,'single'),'CU');dlnet = dlnetwork(reluLayer, exampleInput);save('trainedNet.mat','dlnet');% Define input types to entry-point function 'mpredict'I = randi(255,[5,5]);dlIn1 = dlarray(single(I),'CU');Inputs = {coder.Constant('trainedNet.mat'), dlIn1};% Generate codecfg = coder.gpuConfig('mex');cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('cudnn');codegen mpredict -config cfg -args Inputs
Workaround: Do either of these:When specifying function input types in the codegen command, change the data format from 'CU' to 'CB'.?Change target library from 'cudnn' to 'tensorrt'.?

Summary: Incorrect results might occur for MATLAB code containing loops with the same number of iterations that might conditionally write to the same scalar value
Description: Incorrect code might generate from the MATLAB code that meets all  of these conditions: The code contains for -loops or while  loops or a combination of loops that have the same number of iterations.? The loops write to the same scalar variable. One of the writes is conditional, where the output of the condition varies across loop iterations.?  This happens because of an optimization that incorrectly fuses loops of the above MATLAB coding pattern. Consider this example MATLAB: ? ? ?function y = example(a,b,y)  ? ? ? ? for i = 1:7  ? ? ? ? ? ? y(1).f2 = b(6:1:6,4:1:4);? ? ? ? ? ? ??  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? for j = 1:7  ? ? ? ? ? ? if (a((j) + (6),1)) >= (double(0))  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? y(1).f2(1:1:1,1:1:1) = abs(int16(0));  ? ? ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? end  The for- loops in the MATLAB code have the same number of iterations and both write to y(1).f2 . T he second loop conditionally writes to it based on the if -condition. The generated C code looks like this: ? ?void example(const short a[13], const short b[81], struct0_T y[2])  ? ?{  ? ? short b_i;  ? ? for (int i{0}; i < 7; i++) {  ? ? ? b_i = b[32];  ? ? ? if (a[i + 6] >= 0) {  ? ? ? ? b_i = 0;  ? ? ?} } ? ? y[0].f2 = b_i;  ? ? y[0].f2 = b_i; } The generate code incorrectly fused the MATLAB for -loops which produces incorrect results.

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 A2-7-3 false positive violations on documented class templates
Description: Polyspace incorrectly reports violations of AUTOSAR-C++14 A2-7-3 when class templates are instantiated. False positive violations can occur on the class template declaration or on its function and data members. For example, consider this code: // Instantiated template.  // False Positive  violation template<typename T> class Instance {public:? // Default ctor.  // False Positive  violation ? Instance() {}? ? // A data member usage  // False Positive  violation ? T getVal1() { return val1; }};   Instance<int> ii;? // Instantiation required for Bug Finder analysis  Polyspace raised false positive violations on documented user defined objects.

Summary: Motor & Drive (System-Level) block inaccurately extends tabulated efficiency into other quadrants
Description: If you set the  Parameterize losses by  parameter to  Tabulated efficiency data as a function of speed andtorque   and you provide data for only one or two quadrants?(the values of the  Vector ofspeeds (w) for tabulated losses  or  Vector of torques (T) fortabulated losses  parameters are only positive), the Motor & Drive (System-Level) block extends the full four-quadrant efficiency map inaccurately.  Consequently, if you run a simulation where the motor operates in a quadrant in which you did not specify the efficiency table, the power that the motor dissipates might be inaccurate.
Workaround: The best practice is to provide tabulated loss data as a function of speed and torque, rather than tabulated efficiency data, because:Efficiency becomes ill-defined for zero speed or zero torque.Using losses, you can also account for fixed losses that are still present for zero speed or torque.To fix the quadrant extension issue, provide tabulated loss data instead of efficiency data:Set the?Parameterize losses by?parameter to?Tabulated loss data as a function of speed and torque.Convert the efficiency map into an equivalent loss map. Use the attached function?getExtendedLossTableFromEffTable.m or compute the equivalent loss map from first principles.Set the Corresponding losses, P(w,T)?parameter value to the resulting loss table.To automatically set the block parameters, use the attached function convertEffToLossInMotorBlock.m.In your model, click the Motor & Drive (System-Level) block that you want to convert.At the MATLAB Command Window, enter:? >> convertEffToLossInMotorBlock(gcb)If you set?the?Parameterize losses by?parameter to?Tabulated efficiency data as a function of speed, torque, and DC supply voltage,?you must modify the attached function?convertEffToLossInMotorBlock.m?accordingly.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulating Gain blocks with fixed-point gain parameters may produce incorrect results in Simulink Compiler and RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck set to 'off'
Description: Simulink Compiler and??RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck ?set to? 'off' ?may produce incorrect results if the following conditions are true: The model being simulated contains a Gain block. The gain parameter of the Gain block is an expression that involves one or more variables (for example, "x+y") and is not just the name of a variable (for example, "x"). The "Parameter data type" setting of the Gain block is a fixed-point type (for example, fixdt(1,16) ).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when you select the latch option for a function-call model input port that is in a feedback path
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when all  of these?conditions are met: In the referenced model, there is a Trigger Port block of root level of the model with the?Trigger type ?parameter set to function-call . In the referenced model, there are root-level Inport blocks with the?Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs ?parameter selected. In the top model, the corresponding input port of the Model block is on the feedback signal path.?
Workaround: Clear the Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs?option on the referenced model root level Inport blocks and insert Function-Call Feedback Latch blocks in front of corresponding input ports of the Model block in the top model.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when S-Functions generated by using Legacy Code Tool are configured for supporting row major arrays
Description: MATLAB might crash if an S-Function specification defined by the Legacy Code Tool if all of the following conditions are true: The function specifies vector arguments of type void*  or void **  The option?convertNDArrayToRowMajor ?is set to true?

Summary: HDL Coder produces an error when If-Action Subsystems are used under multiple model reference hierarchies
Description: An error is generated during HDL code generation for a model that contains: More than one model reference block that refer to different models An If-Action Subsystem in more than one of the referenced models  In this case, HDL Coder cannot generate code.

Summary: Error installing HDL Coder Support Package for Microchip FPGA and SoC Devices.
Description: During the installation of HDL Coder Support Package for Microchip FPGA and SoC Devices ?when you click?Setup Now  option on Hardware Support Package (HSP) ?Setup Wizard  dialog box, it throws a following error: Incorrect value for the field fwupdate in the support_package_registry. xml for support package with Base Code:? MICRO_FPGA_SOC?   HSP Setup Wizard  is not supported for Microchip Devices.?
Workaround: Select?Setup Later option appearing on HSP Setup Wizard dialog box.

Summary: Red Non-Terminating Call (NTC) check on stubbed functions with parameters of type "pointer to const structure"
Description: If your code has a stubbed function that takes a pointer to a const  structure, for example, ? void foo(const S*) where S  is a structure containing pointer fields, you might see a red Non-Terminating Call (NTC) check when you call this function with a partially initialized structure. Despite the partial initialization, the red check is not appropriate since the stubbed function might not read the non-initialized fields. Note that a function is stubbed when its body is not available to the Polyspace analysis. You can also explicitly stub functions using the option?-functions-to-stub.  For example: typedef struct {  int x;  int* p;} S;extern void foo(const S*);int main() {     S s;    s.x = 42;    foo(&s);  // Here s.p is not initialized, there is a red check on this call}

Summary: Segmentation violation when updating Simulink model containing multiple signals specified by Simulink.Signal objects driving a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block
Description: A segmentation violation can occur when updating a Simulink model that contains multiple signals specified by Simulink.Signal  objects driving a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block. ?Your model might be affected when all the following conditions are true: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem such as enabled, triggered, or function-call subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystem contains an Outport block with Initial output  set to []  (empty matrix). The Outport block is driven by a Mux block or a virtual Bus Creator block. The source signal for the second input and above of the Mux block or virtual Bus Creator block has a Simulink.Signal  object associated with it. The storage class (CoderInfo.StorageClass ) of the Simulink.Signal  object in the model workspace has a value other than 'Auto' . Initial value (InitialValue) ?of the Simulink.Signal  object is not empty.  A segmentation violation is more likely with higher number of input ports for the Mux block or virtual Bus Creator block.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Generated code with large identifiers can be incompatible with generated code test generation
Description: When the generated code for a model is containing C identifiers with more than 63 chars, generated code test generation is only using the first 63 chars to identify them. This can cause an error if different identifiers are truncated to the same name, causing the model to be incompatible.  That error will be reported as An internal error occurred during the code analysis  before the incompatibility message.
Workaround: A workaround is to reduce the length of the identifiers in the generated code so that they are less than 63 chars long. This can be done by:Reducing the value of Maximum identifier length in the Code Generation / Identifiers setting, and/orUsing a shorter name for the model name

Summary: Model Advisor check mathworks.simulink.MLFBOutputDimensions errors for MATLAB Function blocks with no Function blocks in the top model
Description: When you try to upgrade a model from R2017a to R2022b using the Upgrade Advisor, the check Check output dimensions of MATLAB Function blocks  (Check ID:?mathworks.simulink.MLFBOutputDimensions ) errors when the? MATLAB Function blocks have no Function blocks in the top model. The error message is similar to:Abnormal Exit: Unable to perform assignment because value of type 'Stateflow.EMChart' is not convertible to 'double'

Summary: Simulating Simulink models through code generation may fail when using MinGW compiler and?non-ASCII characters for paths
Description: Building Simulink models may fail when you simulate your model through code generation, use a MinGW compiler, and the model or the model dependencies (for example, custom code) are inside a path with non-ASCII characters.? For example, if a Simulink model myModel.slx  is simulated through code generation and is placed in a directory with Japanese name:??ČŐ±ľŐZĄŐĄ©ĄëĄŔ/MyModel.slx , the following error is thrown to the diagnostic viewer: gcc: error: c:\users\abc\appdata\local\temp\g2915480\???????\data_array.c: Invalid argumentgcc: fatal error: no input filescompilation terminated  This issue is because MinGW does not work well with non-ASCII path.?
Workaround: If you have compilers other than MinGW, please change the compiler using the command:??mex -setup

Summary: Data does not update in deployed DSP System Toolbox scope applications
Description: When you run a deployed application (.exe, .app) that contains timescope, dsp.ArrayPlot, or spectrumAnalyzer objects, the data on the visual does not update for these post simulation operations: 1) Zooming or panning on the axes 2) Clicking on the Fit-to-view button  3) Turning on measurements
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 4, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Selecting?Remove root level I/O zero initialization yields incorrect code when both top and bottom models have Merge blocks connected to outports and the outport of the bottom model is connected to the Merge block in the top model
Description: Selecting?Remove root level I/O zero initialization  yields incorrect code generation?when?all ?of the following are true:The top model has a Merge block with a zero initial output The top model Merge block is connected to an outport The top model has a bottom model whose outport is connected to the top model Merge block The bottom model has a Merge block with a nonzero initial output The bottom model Merge block is connected to an outport  For example, consider this code, generated with?Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?unselected: ?/* Model initialize function */  void top_initialize(void)  {        /* SystemInitialize for ModelReference: '<Root>/Model' incorporates:        *  Outport: '<Root>/Outport_top'        */       bot_Init(&top_Y.Outport_top);       /* SystemInitialize for Outport: '<Root>/Outport_top' incorporates:        *  Merge: '<Root>/Merge'        */       top_Y.Outport_top = 0.0;          }?    Compare to this incorrect code, generated with Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?selected: void top_initialize(void)   {    /* SystemInitialize for ModelReference: '<Root>/Model' incorporates:    * Outport: '<Root>/Outport_top'    */    bot_Init(&top_Y.Outport_top);     }?  In the incorrect code, the function bot_Init  might set?top_Y.Outport_top  to a nonzero value. This yields different results from the code generated with Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?selected.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Use slrealtime.getCrashStack  to decode the core file. matlabroot, 'toolbox', 'physmod', 'simscape', 'engine', 'sli', 'lib'), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'toolbox', 'physmod', 'simscape', 'engine', 'core', 'lib'), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'toolbox', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'pm', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'pm_math', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'sm', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'sm_ssci', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'ssc_core', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'ssc_sli', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'ssc_st', 'lib' ), ...Find the R2023a static libraries here:fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'ex', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'mc', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot, 'extern', 'physmod', 'win64', 'pm', 'lib' ), ...fullfile( matlabroot,
