Summary: Code mapping configuration for Outport blocksis lost when the model is closed
Description: If a model contains Outport blocks that aredefined in a custom library, the configuration for these blocks in the code mapping are lost when the model is closed.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Unexpected behavior for a variable-size array of buses whose type is specified without 'Bus:' prefix
Description: When you specify the type of a Stateflow.Data as a bus type without including the "Bus:" prefix, Stateflow does not honor?Variable size  property of that data. The data is always treated as fixed size.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect backfolding of variable assignment into function calls
Description: In a MATLAB function, when a global variable is declared and used in only one MATLAB function and the functions that it calls, it might be converted to a local variable and passed into the called function as an input. Code generation might incorrectly fold back the assignment that copies the value of the variable into the call site of the called function. This incorrect backfolding results in a change of evaluation order and incorrect results. For example, this MATLAB function calls the function callee : function out = caller(in1, in2)?  ? ? global P2. ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?// (1) Declaration of global variable P2  ? ? [P1] = callee(in1,in2);? ? ? ?// (2) Callsite of function callee , which directly access global P2  ? ? P1(1,1) = P2;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?// (3) Copy of P2  end?  ?In the generated code, the variable P2  is converted to a local variable and passed into callee  as an input in statement (2). The copy of P2  in statement (3) is folded back to the callsite (2) and statement (3) is eliminated. The logic in the the generated code is: ? ? [P1] = callee(in1, in2, P(1, 1))  ?This optimization incorrectly changes the evaluation order of P(1, 1)  .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a masked subsystem inside a For Each  iterator block The configuration parameter Enable local block outputs  is selected

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong simulation results with model reference in accelerator mode containing Variant Subsystems with startup activation
Description: Variant Subsystems with startup activation in the referenced model might cause wrong simulation results when all below conditions are met.Model block referring to another model containing Variant Subsystem in startup activation time? Simulation mode set to accelerator? Variant Subsystem choices have inconsistent interface. For example, the first variant choice may have two outports and second choice may have only outport. The issue may not be reproducible if we have all variant choices with same number of outports. The Variant Subsystem choices must have outports with branched sources with no other non-virtual blocks connecting between them.?  For example: variant choice may contain these blocks:? In1 -> Out1?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?-> Out2??  ?Where In1 is Inport and Out1, Out2 are outports for the variant choice blocks.
Workaround: Add a signal conversion for the branched source inside the variant choice blocks.For example,? if your variant choice block contain these blocks:? ? ? ? ? ? In1 -> Out1?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? -> Out2? ??? ? ? ? ? ? add a signal conversion between In1 and Out1 or Out2?? ? ? ? ? ?? ? ? ? ? ?In1 -> Out1?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? - Signal Conversion -> Out2? ??
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when reusable subsystem inlines a function call from referenced model
Description: The code generator produces incorrect code when all ?of these conditions are met:  The top model contains a Reusable Subsystem. The top model is configured with Code interface packaging  set to Nonreusable function .? The Reusable Subsystem outputs more than one function call.? One of the output function calls is connected to an inport of a referenced model.
Workaround: The workaround for this bug is to convert the function packaging of the reusable subsystem to 'Inline'. To do this, right-click on the reusable subsystem and select 'Properties'. In the 'Subsystem Properties' dialog box, select the 'Function Packaging' property and select 'Inline' from the drop-down list.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Conversion from subsystem to referenced model fails with "Conversion to logical from cell is not possible" error
Description: Converting an atomic subsystem to a referenced model produces an unexpected error when all these conditions apply: A Data Store Memory block outside of the subsystem defines a data store that contains multidimensional data. The subsystem contains at least one Data Store Read or Data Store Write block that accesses the data in the data store. The Data Store Read or Data Store Write block has multiple ports that correspond with partial elements of the data store.  The error message is: Conversion to logical from cell is not possible.
Workaround: Before converting the subsystem to a referenced model, move all blocks that correspond with the data store into the subsystem.

Summary: View and Commit Changes?dialog does not open from the Source Control menu
Description: When using Source Control, it is possible to get MATLAB into a state where the View and Commit Changes ?dialog does not open. This can happen after the View and Commit Changes ?dialog is opened and dismissed.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, close the View and Commit Changes?dialog only by clicking Cancel in the dialog rather than the X in its banner.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect signal output for bus element port driven by Initialize Function block or Terminate Function block in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Simulink does not log the signal correctly and produces incorrect output for a bus element port in rapid accelerator simulation mode when both of these conditions apply: Bus element port is driven by Initialize Function block or Terminate Function block. Bus element port is driven by another block with a sample time different from the Initialize Function block or Terminate Function block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Error during test generation analysis for models containing S-functions with boolean inputs or outputs
Description: Test generation analysis fails and throws following error when a model contains an S-function with boolean inputs or outputs: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end .

Summary: Calling the sign function with NaN as input may return a NaN with a different bit pattern
Description: Calling the sign  function with NaN  as input may return a NaN  with a different bit pattern. The output is still a valid representation of NaN  but it has a different bit pattern than the input NaN  value. To reproduce this behavior, start a fresh session of MATLAB then execute the following code. Note that the value at the fifth iteration of the loop is different from the value displayed by the previous four.for iter = 1:5s = sign(NaN);fprintf("Bit pattern of sign(NaN) is %bx and isnan(sign(NaN)) returns %s.\n", s, string(isnan(s)))end Bit pattern of sign(NaN) is fff8000000000000 and isnan(sign(NaN)) returns true.  Bit pattern of sign(NaN) is fff8000000000000 and isnan(sign(NaN)) returns true.  Bit pattern of sign(NaN) is fff8000000000000 and isnan(sign(NaN)) returns true.  Bit pattern of sign(NaN) is fff8000000000000 and isnan(sign(NaN)) returns true.  Bit pattern of sign(NaN) is ffffffffffffffff and isnan(sign(NaN)) returns true.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling initialize and terminate functions does not clear persistent variable state if this variable is accessed inside parfor loop
Description: The state of persistent variables in standalone generated code is usually cleared after calling the initialize and terminate functions. For example, in the following code snippet, the second call to foo  runs the program with a clean state of persistent variables and behaves identically to the first call to foo : int main(void) {  foo_initialize()  foo();  foo_terminate()   foo_initialize()  foo();  foo_terminate()} For more details on this behavior, see https://www.mathworks.com/help/coder/ug/working-with-persistent-variables.html#WorkingWithPersistentVariablesExample-7 . If a persistent variable is accessed inside a parfor  loop, then the state of that variable is not cleared after calling the initialize and terminate functions. This behavior is a bug and might cause the generated code to product incorrect answers. For example, define this MATLAB entry-point function foo  and subfunction?incrementPersistent : function y = fooy = incrementPersistent(0);parfor idx = 1:100    incrementPersistent(0);endendfunction result = incrementPersistent(x)persistent pif isempty(p)    p = x;endp = p + 1;result = p;end Define this C main function that calls the generated entry-point C function: #include "foo.h"#include "foo_initialize.h"#include "foo_terminate.h"#include <stdio.h>int main() {    foo_initialize();    printf("Result from first instance: %f\n", foo());    foo_terminate();    foo_initialize();    printf("Result from second instance: %f\n", foo());    foo_terminate();} Generate an executable by running these commands:? cfg = coder.config('exe');cfg.GenerateExampleMain = 'DoNotGenerate';cfg.CustomSource = 'main.c';codegen foo -config cfg -report The generated executable produces this output: Result from first instance: 1. 000000Result from second instance: 2. 000000 This behavior is incorrect. The result from the second call to foo ?must be identical to the result of the first call to foo ?because all persistent state must be cleared by the calls to terminate and initialize.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variable-size output signal might not be properly initialized in generated code or Rapid Accelerator
Description: A variable-size output signal, connected to a root outport block, might be incorrectly initialized in generated code or when simulating in Rapid Accelerator mode if the signal originates in a conditionally executed subsystem. This might happen if the output value of the conditionally executed subsystem is held and the execution of the subsystem is not triggered during the step function. In releases prior to R2011a, this bug also affects legacy products Stateflow Coder, Link for TASKING, Link for Code Composer Studio, and the various Embedded Targets and Target Support Packages.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Code generation fails with an error of Object no longer exists.
Description: When you generate a code generation report during code generation, code generation fails and produces this error: "Object no longer exists"
Workaround: At the command line, run this command:Simulink.ID.repair(modelName)Regenerate the code after you run the command.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rapid-accelerator mode simulation or code generation might produce wrong answer for certain blocks in a referenced model containing message blocks
Description: Rapid-accelerator mode simulation or code generation might produce incorrect results for a model hierarchy that meets all  of these conditions:? The referenced model contains blocks with constant rate or reinitialize (or reset) event The referenced model contains a Model block with message ports or a Message Merge block with different input rates The referenced model is not an export-function model or software architecture model  The blocks with constant rate or reinitialize/reset events do not run as expected and might produce a default or unexpected value.
Workaround: In the referenced model, take one of the following actions:Remove the connection and signal logging on the message ports of Model blocks. Also remove the connection of Message Merge block with different input rates.Remove the blocks with constant rate or reinitialize/reset events.

Summary: Wrong answer in accelerator mode simulation for models with muxed asynchronous function-call inputs that call a referenced export-function model
Description: Accelerator mode simulation may give incorrect results when all of the following conditions are met: There is at least a 3-level model reference hierarchy The mid level referenced model has at least 2 asynchronous function-call inputs that are muxed  together to form a wide function-call signal The muxed? function-call signal is connected to a function-call port of the bottom level export-function model

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size of variable-size struct field incorrectly modified prior to generated code that reads and writes the same data
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code when all ?of these are true: An assignment statement completely writes a variable-size struct field The right-hand side of the assignment statement contains an access of a single element of the same variable-size struct field using a subscript  MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for the statement. The generated code modifies the size of the variable-size struct field prior to the code that reads the variable-size data. This causes incorrect behavior when the read of the variable-size struct field also uses the modified size to compute a subscript. The generated code might read uninitialized memory or incorrect numeric results. The attached example ReproductionModel.zip  demonstrates this issue. This code snippet from the example shows the coding pattern described above: s.f1 = s.f1(2,2) - temp;   See this corresponding generated code: i = s->f1->size[0] * s->f1->size[1];  s->f1->size[0] = temp->size[0];  s->f1->size[1] = 1;  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(sp, s->f1, i, &d_emlrtRTEI);  b_s = s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1];  for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) {  ? s->f1->data[i] = b_s - temp_data[i];  }   The expression? s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1] ?in the generated code?corresponds to the MATLAB expression? s.f1(2,2) . Note that the size of the struct field  s->f1  has been incorrectly modified prior to this expression. The generated code should update the size of  s->f1 ?after that expression.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Deployed application crashes when instantiating a .NET delegate with a MATLAB function handle when that delegate is invoked from a background .NET thread
Description: When a MATLAB function is used to instantiate a .NET delegate, invoking that delegate from a .NET background thread will cause a crash when the MATLAB application is deployed. Consider the following C# class: namespace MyNamespace {? // A .NET delegate that returns void and accepts no arguments.?? public delegate void MyDelegate();?? public class MyClass {?? ? // A public field of MyClass, of type MyDelegate.? ? public MyDelegate Delegate;? ? // A public method that invokes the Delegate field from a background thread.? ? public void InvokeDelegate() {? ? ? Thread t = new Thread(new ThreadStart(() =>?Delegate.Invoke()));? ? ? t.IsBackground = true;? ? ? t.Start();? ?? }???}} In MATLAB, MyClass  might be used like the following: >> NET.addAssembly("C:\temp\MyAssembly.dll"); % Add the assembly to MATLAB>> cls = MyNamespace.MyClass();? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?% Instantiate MyClass>> callback = @()disp("Delegate invoked!");? ?% Define the callback>> cls.Delegate = callback;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?% Set MyClass.Delegate to a function handle?>> cls.InvokeDelegate();? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? % Invoke the function handle from .NET?  When this code is deployed using MATLAB Compiler or MATLAB Compiler SDK, MATLAB will crash on line 5. The error will contain the following lines: Unhandled Exception: System.Runtime.InteropServices.SEHException: External component has thrown an exception.?? ?at dotnetcli.postAsyncCallEventAndWait(NETFunctionHandleCallData* ) ?? ?at dotnetcli.NETDelegateInvocationTarget.callbackHandler(ArrayList argList)
Workaround: Starting in R2018a, users can leverage .NET events to invoke MATLAB function handles. The example code can be refactored like the following:public namespace MyNamespace {? public class MyClass {?? ? // A public event with standard event signature.? ? public event EventHandler Event;?? ? // A public method that invokes Event.? ? public void InvokeEvent()?{? ? ? Thread t = new Thread(new ThreadStart(() =>?Event.Invoke(this, EventArgs.Empty)));? ? ? t.IsBackground = true;? ? ? t.Start();? ??}?? }}This class can be used like the following:?>> NET.addAssembly("C:\temp\MyAssembly.dll");? % Add the assembly to MATLAB>> cls = MyNamespace.MyClass();? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? % Instantiate MyClass>> callback = @(~,~)disp("Event invoked!");? ? % Define the callback>> addlistener(cls, "Event", callback);? ? ? ? % Set MyClass.Event to a function handle>> cls.InvokeEvent();? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? % Invoke the function handle from .NETNote that MATLAB only supports standard event signatures. See?Use .NET Events in MATLAB?for details on using events.R2017a and R2017b do not have workarounds. .NET delegates that call into MATLAB cannot be invoked asynchronously.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated MEX file might erroneously accept sparse matrix inputs causing incorrect results or a MATLAB crash
Description: Suppose that you:? Generate a MEX file that accepts a full double or logical array as input.? For example, you run:?codegen myFunction -args {zeros(10,11)} )    Try to pass a sparse input to the generated MEX file for the corresponding argument. For example, you run:?myFunction_mex(sparse(10,11)) ) ?   In many situations, these actions produce an expected error that says that the generated MEX file does not accept sparse inputs. However, in some situations, this error might not be produced and you might see unexpected behavior or a MATLAB crash.
Workaround: Make sure you pass inputs of the expected sparsity to the generated MEX file by doing either of these: Convert the input value from sparse to full in MATLAB before passing it to the generated MEX, or Specify a sparse input type in the -args option when using the?codegen?command to generate the MEX.

Summary: Error while using TI C2000 XCP CAN Monitor and Tune for PEAK Systems CAN adapters
Description: The current system errors out with XCP on CAN TI C2000 boards setup, specifically with PEAK Systems CAN adapters. Please note that this issue prevents the execution of External mode  on CAN in Tl C2000 boards for PEAK system CAN adapters.The incorrect behavior is seen with the following steps: Navigate to?Configuration settings > Hardware Implementation > Hardware board settings > Target hardware resources . Select?External mode ?under?Groups . Select?XCP on CAN ?under?External mode > Communication interface ?and click?Apply . Specify?Peak-System ?under?CAN vendor ?and click?Apply.  The following error message appears:?The value 'PEAK-System' is an invalid entry for the 'External mode' field 'CANVendor'.  Specify?PCAN_USBBUS1 ?under?CAN device ?and click Apply . The following error message appears: The value 'PCAN_USBBUS1' is an invalid entry for the 'External Mode' field 'CANDevice'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect array indexing in a for-loop for discontiguous signals
Description: The generated code for a Simulink block or S-function might use an incorrect for -loop index instead of producing an error when all of the following conditions are true: The block has a discontiguous input signal. The block has a tunable parameter that uses a field of a structure variable.
Workaround: Pass the block input signal through a Signal Conversion block that has the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter selected.If you are using an S-function with custom TLC code and Simulink R2023b or later, use the enhanced TLC interface.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder produces incorrect code when the inputs to?mldivide or mrdivide?function?are of different data types
Description: The mldivide  ("\ ") and?mrdivide  ("/ ") functions calculate the root of linear equations such?x = A\B and  x = B/A.  However, GPU coder might generate incorrect code for the?mldivide  or mrdivide ?function when all  of these conditions are true: Linear equation?Ax=B ?or  xA=B  does not have a solution Inputs A  and B  are of different data types

Summary: Polyspace analysis might not recognize threads created through lambda functions or std::function objects
Description: If you use Polyspace concurrency detection to determine your multitasking model, the analysis might not recognize C++ threads created using std::thread  where you pass a lambda function as an argument. For example, this code snippet creates a thread lambda_thread  which writes a value of 1 to global variable gvar . std::thread lambda_thread([]? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? { gvar = 1; });  When you analyze the previous code, Polyspace does not recognize the thread creation and does not analyze the code inside the lambda function. As a result:  A Bug Finder analysis might not report all DATA_RACE  defects in your code.? A Code Prover analysis does not consider global variable gvar  as shared. You specify option -enable-concurrency-detection  to to enable concurrency detection with Code Prover.?
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Code generation might fail for a model passing a Simulink.LookupTable object as a model argument
Description: Code generation might fail when a model passes a Simulink.LookupTable  object as a model argument. In this case, the code generator reports an error similar to this:?Code generation assertion 'orgCoderGroupName. empty()' failed in 'B:\ matlab\ src\ slcg_coderdi_impl\ server\ CoderDIServerImpl. cpp:552'?
Workaround: Change the Simulink.LookupTable argument to two separate parameter arguments.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Crash during ASAP2 file export from Simulink
Description: When exporting ASAP2 files from Simulink, the code generation process might crash and display the following line in the initial stack frame: coder::descriptor::StructExpression::Impl::getBaseVariable?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect access of variables that?use the?coder::array?template?with Dynamic Memory Allocation for Fixed-Size Arrays (DynamicMemoryAllocationForFixedSizeArrays) enabled
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code from your MATLAB code that meets all   of these conditions: Your MATLAB code uses the reshape  function. Dynamic Memory Allocation for Fixed-Size Arrays  (DynamicMemoryAllocationForFixedSizeArrays ) is enabled.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: function [IA6] = set0001_funToCall(IA4,IA6)? ? IA6{1} = [IA6{1},...? ? min(+IA4(4:1:20,8:1:13),...? ? bitand(IA4(1:1:17,2:3:17),reshape(repmat(IA6{1}(4,3),102,1),[17,6])))];end  The code generated from the MATLAB function?set0001_funToCall ?is shown below:  ?coder::array<short, 2U> r2;  ? coder::array<short, 1U> r1;    r2 = r1. reshape(17,  6);?? ? coder::internal::minimum2(b_IA4, r, r2);?  ? r1[0][0]; The generated code writes the result of the reshape ?function to a temporary variable r2, ?which performs a deep copy of r1 . The subsequent calling coder::internal::minimum2 ?operation on the temporary variable r2  is meant to modify shared memory between r1  and r2 ?but does not occur because of the previous deep copy of r1 . As a result, r1  contains incorrect data.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink based states in a Stateflow chart might not be enabled/disabled when the chart is enabled/disabled
Description: Stateflow might produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true:? The chart is located inside a conditionally executed subsystem like an enabled subsystem or a function call subsystem The chart contains a Simulink based state. Simulink functions in Stateflow charts are not affected by this bug. The Simulink based state itself contains a modeling pattern that depends on knowing whether or not it has previously been disabled

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you interact with components that have physical ports in a System Composer model
Description: Creating a subsystem from a System Composer?component that has at least two physical ports, when at least one of the ports is assigned to a physical interface,?might cause MATLAB to crash.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Logged data might be incorrect for signals in direct feedthrough path of subsystem or referenced model configured to minimize algebraic loops
Description: When you select the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences ?parameter for a Subsystem block or a Model block, logged data for signals in the direct feedthrough path inside the subsystem or referenced model might be incorrect. Only logged values are incorrect. The simulation uses correct signal values.?

Summary: Subsystem blocks with different variant conditions may lead to unused variables in generated code
Description: Subsystem blocks with different variant conditions may lead to unused variables when all below conditions are met ?Model is configured to generate pre-preprocessor condition for variant blocks through ert targets. ?Variant Source  or Sink  blocks connected to Subsystem inputs. ?Variant Source  or Sink  blocks present inside and outside of subsystem ?Subsystem has looser condition compared to itˇŻs ports  Example:                  +-------------------------------------------+                 |                                           |                 |              +---\        +---+           |   (In1)-------->|              |    \       |   |           |                         |   (In1)------|B==1|-------|   |           |    +---\                  |              |    /       |   |           |    |    \                 |              +---/        |   |---(Out1)  |----|A==1|-------(Out1)                 |               VS          |   |           |    |    /                 |   (In2)-------------------|   |           |    +---/                  |                           |   |           |     VS1      (In2)-------->|                           +---+           |                 |                            Sum            |                 |                                           |                 +-------------------------------------------+                                   Subsystem In the above arrangement VS and VS1 can be Variant Source  blocks with B==1 and A==1 conditions. During model compilation Subsystem  will get a condition A==1, Subsystem.In1 will get B==1 condition. The input port corresponding to Subsystem.In1 gets a condition A==1 && B==1. In this case when? B~=1 there will be an unused variable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for properties?function returns incorrect list of names because return value includes names of hidden properties
Description: Code generated for the properties ? function returns an incorrect list of names.?This return value contains names of hidden properties of the class, in addition to the non-hidden properties. In MATLAB execution, the return value of this function does not include the names of hidden properties. For example, suppose that the class MyClass  has one public property pub  and one hidden property hidden .?  Given an instance?obj ?of?MyClass , calling?properties(obj) ?in MATLAB returns ['pub'] . ?However, in code generation, it returns ['pub', 'hidden'] .? This issue affects MATLAB Coder and Simulink (MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant blocks may cause incorrect initial condition propagation leading to mismatch in simulation results with different variant activation times
Description: Variant blocks such as a Variant Subsystem ?may cause incorrect initial condition propagation when all below conditions are met:Variant block such as a Variant Subsystem  block present in conditionally executed systems such as enabled subsystem. Variant choice blocks have different initial conditions specified on outports of conditional subsystems present inside variant choices. Outport blocks with initial condition have Output when disabled  set to reset .  Switching variant activation from update diagram  to update diagram analyze all choices or other variant activation  can lead to incorrect initial condition propagation from variant blocks leading to a mismatch in simulation results.

Summary: Compilation error when simulating a model with a custom code function that uses an enumeration type
Description: Simulink throws a custom code compilation error when simulating a model where all of the following conditions are true: The Simulation target language  is set to C++ in Configuration Parameters ?>  Simulation Target . An imported enumeration type is used in the input or output of a custom code function? This function is called using a C Caller or C Function block  The error message mentions an invalid type conversion involving the enumeration type.

Summary: "Use of enumeration type" error when MATLAB Function calls interp1
Description: If you call?interp1  function from a Matlab Function in a Stateflow Chart using C action language, Simulink throws an unexpected error: Use of enumeration type coder.internal.interpolate.interpMethodsEnum in Chart <chartName> is not allowed because the datatype could not be successfully registered with Simulink.
Workaround: Convert the chart to use MATLAB action language or move the function to a chart with MATLAB action language and export it.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A MATLAB Function Block that uses in-place optimization with a variable-size signal produces incorrect code
Description: If a MATLAB Function block changes the dimensions or size of an in-place variable size signal, the code generator incorrectly does not adjust the dimensions of the output.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code behaves incorrectly when a tunable parameter of type single or double is cast to an integer type
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when a block specifies a tunable parameter expression that consists of casting a tunable parameter of data type single or double to an integer type. ?For example, if a Constant block were to have as its value the expression "int8(P) " where P  is of data type single or double, then? simulation will round the value of P  to the nearest integer (for example, 3.6 is rounded to 4) code will truncate the value of P  to the nearest integer that is less than P  (for example, 3.6 is truncated to 3)
Workaround: The behavior of the code will be correct if you add a superfluous cast to the tunable parameter expression.? For example, if a Constant block were to have the expression "int8(P)" where?P is of data type single or double, then add a superfluous cast to the expression so that it is "int8(int8(P))"

Summary: Message storage blocks do not clear their storage as expected inside a subsystem or model that resets or reinitializes states
Description: Message storage blocks do not clear their storage or reset states as expected when they are inside any ?of these: A subsystem or a model containing a Reinitialize Function block A conditionally executed subsystem or a model that is configured to reset states, such as a Resettable Subsystem block or an Enabled Subsystem block that is configured to reset its state when enabling  These are the message storage blocks with the unexpected behavior: Queue block Stateflow Chart with internal input messages Receive block configured with internal queue Entity Transport Delay block Auto-inserted queue (shown as a queue badge) on the input port of a Receive block, Stateflow Chart with external input message, or MATLAB System block, and the message sender is also inside the same component SimEvents blocks with storages such as Entity Server

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may report a model as incompatible if any referenced model has the diagnostics option?Automatic solver parameter selection?set to error
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may report a model as incompatible if any of its referenced models have the Diagnostics option?Automatic solver parameter selection ? set to?error . An example error message: Unable to determine a fixed step size based on the sample times in the model 'ReferencedModel', because the model does not have any discrete sample times. You can disable this diagnostic by explicitly specifying a fixed step size in the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, or setting the 'Automatic solver parameter selection' diagnostic to 'none' in the Solver group on the Diagnostics pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: On all referenced models, set?Automatic solver parameter selection to?warning or none,?before invoking Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: Excessive model compile time when certain Simscape blocks are present
Description: When a model contains certain Simscape blocks, the compile time after clicking Run  can be very long. The issue stems from the index reduction algorithms taking an excessively long time. The affected blocks include (but are not limited to): System-Level Refrigeration Cycle (2P) System-Level Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) System-Level Condenser Evaporator (2P-TL) System-Level Heat Exchanger (2P-2P) System-Level Heat Exchanger (TL-MA)
Workaround: Turn off index reduction in affected models by opening the Solver Configuration block and setting Index reduction method to None.

Summary: Incorrect frame origin location in the File Solid block when using Based on Geometric Feature
Description: In the File Solid block, when creating anew frame on a surface by selecting the Based on Geometric Feature ?check box, the frame must be located at thecentroid of the selected surface. However, for certain imported geometries, the created frame is located at an incorrect location.?

Summary: Possible MATLAB crash when simulating modally reduced flexible beams
Description: MATLAB might crash when simulating and visualizing a Simscape Multibody model that contains a flexible beam block configured to be modally reduced with a nonzero number of retained modes.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can disable graphical display of modally reduced flexible beam blocks by selecting None?for their?Graphic >?Type?parameter.One possible strategy is to enable display for the beam but specify its Number of Retained Modes parameter to 0 while building the model. Later, when the model is ready for simulation, you can disable the beam's graphics and specify a non-zero number of retained modes to capture flexible behavior.

Summary: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks gives abnormal exit warning for Saturation Dynamic blocks.
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks?(ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0628) ?gives abnormal exit warning for Saturation Dynamic  blocks when upper or lower saturation limit inputs are not connected.

Summary: Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that new version of the source document is available after opening requirement set from Git repository
Description: When you use MATLAB to clone a Git repository and then open an imported requirement set in the Requirements Editor, the Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that a new version of the source document in available. Similarly, when you pull the latest changes for an imported requirement set from Git and open it, the Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that a new version of the source document is available.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Adding the resources folder for custom tabs to the MATLAB path and then calling slReloadToolstripConfig does not add the custom tabs to the Simulink Toolstrip
Description: The first time you call slReloadToolstripConfig  in a script, Simulink might not add custom tabs to the toolstrip. For example, this code might not add custom tabs to the toolstrip: function addCustomTab(folder)? ? ?addpath(folder);? ? ?slReloadToolstripConfig;end
Workaround: Add the attached P-file to the MATLAB path. Instead of?the?addpath() or rmpath()?command, use this command:workaroundFor02844522(pathToParentFolder, Operation =operation);The input argument?pathToParentFolder?is theabsolute path to theparent folder of the resources folder. The input argument?operation?iseither?'add'?or?'remove'.For example:?function addCustomTab(myPath)? ? ?workaroundFor02844522(myPath, Operation ='add');? ? ?slReloadToolstripConfig;end

Summary: Unexpected compilation errors in shared utility code generated for Stateflow library charts
Description: When compiling generated code for library charts, an error similar to the following might be thrown: rtwshared. lib modelName_rtwlib. lib(ex_model3. obj) : error LNK2019: unresolved external symbol modelName_lib_chart_during referenced in function modelName . . \ referencedModel_ssreuse. exe : fatal error LNK1120: 1 unresolved externals NMAKE : fatal error U1077  Different compilers on other platfiorms may produce slightly different errors.
Workaround: On the library block, specify Function name options as User specified and provide a unique identifier for Function Name.?

Summary: Deployed simulation incorrectly issues a sample time error for top-level Inport blocks that load data in structure without time format
Description: The software incorrectly issues an error about the sample time for top-level Inport blocks if:? The model has the Input ?parameter selected to load external input data using top-level Inport blocks. The external input data is specified using the Structure without time ?logging format, in which the value of the  time ?field is empty ( [] ).  Top-level Inport blocks that load the data have discrete sample time. The simulation runs by calling the sim ?function in a deployed context, using Simulink Compiler, or with the  RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck ?name-value argument specified as  off . ?  When all of these conditions are met, the software incorrectly issues this error: Error loading external data into root-level Inport myModel/u in model 'myModel'.  Caused by:   Invalid 'Sample time' for root level Inport block. When the external input corresponding to an Inport does not specify time values, 'Sample time' must be discrete. ?

Summary: When trying to set the color of markings and other objects, users are unable to set colors with the mouse in the Set Color dialog
Description: In all workflows where a user needs to set a custom color, the color gradient and lightness sliders update their values whenever the mouse is hovered over them, with no way to lock in a color.
Workaround: Users can still use the red, green, and blue color fields on the Set Color dialog box to set a color in all instances of using the Color field of the Attributes Panel.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results for an export function model that references a sample time independent model
Description: Simulating an export-function model in normal mode produces incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true: The model references a sample time independent sub-model inside a function call subsystem. The referenced sub-model contains an enabled subsystem that is enabled by a signal running at constant sample time. The enabled subsystem contains a function call subsystem with an inherited sample time. The model and sub-model have different fixed step sizes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In some charts with "Execute (enter) chart at initialization" enabled, the queueing and dequeuing behavior of messages may lead to unpredictable results
Description: The queueing and dequeuing behavior of messages may lead to unpredictable results in models where all of the following conditions are true: One or more charts have the property "Execute (enter) chart at initialization" enabled. One or more of the charts with the above criteria interact with messages as part of a default transition or entry action of the first state.

Summary: Coverage data aggregation for multiple subsystem harnesses with a top model harness causes error
Description: If you have multiple subsystem test harnesses and a top-level model harness, and run tests using the Test Manager in Simulink Test, coverage data aggregation for all three harnesses might result in an error.  For example, assume you have two subsystem harnesses of the same subsystem, U1 and?U2, and a top model harness, T. Aggregating coverage data for U1 + U2 is compatible Aggregating coverage data for?U1 + T is compatible Aggregating coverage data for?U2 + T is compatible Aggregating coverage data for?U1 + U2 + T is not compatible and causes an error that looks like:  ? ? ? ? ? ?The structure of model "modelName" is incompatible with the coverage data collected on model "harnessModelName".

Summary: Generating standalone code for function that returns variable-size struct might cause MATLAB crash
Description: MATLAB might crash during standalone code generation if both  these conditions are true: One of your entry-point MATLAB functions returns a variable-size struct In the code configuration options, you: Set?Generate example main  (GenerateExampleMain ) to Do not generate an example main function ?('DoNotGenerate' )  Enable Preserve array dimensions ?( PreserveArrayDimensions )    For example, consider this entry-point MATLAB function: function x = example(u)x = u;end Attempting to generate code by running these commands causes a MATLAB crash: x.a = coder.typeof(0,[3 5],1);t = coder.typeof(x) ;cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.PreserveArrayDimensions = true;cfg.GenerateExampleMain = 'DoNotGenerate';codegen -config cfg example -args t -report

Summary: Indexing into tables in an anonymous function can error if used with clear classes or clear all.
Description: Indexing into tables in an anonymous function can error if used with clear classes or clear all. It is essential to run the reproduction steps in a loop.Here is an example that illustrates the problem: function test  ? for i = 1:2  ? ? t = table({1;2;3});?? ?fn = @()t{:,1}{:};?? ?sum(fn());??? clear all;  ? end?end  Executing test function errors:? >> test  Error using? {}?  Cannot find function "@()t{:,1}{:}" within "test.m".  Error in test>@()t{:,1}{:} (line 9)  ? ? fn = @()t{:,1}{:};  Error in test (line 10)  ? ? sum(fn());?
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:1. Clear specific variables instead of using 'clear all' or 'clear classes'. Here's how the updated code for the example function "test.m" looks like:function test? for i = 1:2? ? t = table({1;2;3});?? ?fn = @()t{:,1}{:};?? ?sum(fn());??? clear t;? ? clear fn;??? end?end??2. Avoid indexing into tables in an anonymous function. Creating a named function fixes the issue. Here's the updated code:function testfor i = 1:2? ? t = table({1;2;3});? ? fn = @testHelper;? ? sum(fn(t))? ? clear all;endendfunction varargout = testHelper(t)[varargout{1:nargout}] = t{:,1}{:};end

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models with Specialized Power Systems blocks in Discrete mode
Description: MATLAB might crash and point to the sfun_discreteVariableDelay crash log if all  these conditions are true:? Your Simscape Electrical?model uses blocks from the Specialized Power Systems library You set the?Simulation type ?parameter of the Powergui block to Discrete You simulate the model with the Fast restart option enabled

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from Bit-wise AND operation between signed integers might be incorrect
Description: The generated code might be incorrect code for a MATLAB function or Simulink model that meets the following conditions: The Casting mode ?parameter is set ?to?Standards Compliant . The Bit-wise AND operation is performed between signed integers using the Bitand function or the Bitwise block.  In the generated code, the signed integer is converted to an unsigned type before performing the Bit-wise operation, which is incorrect.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash or produce an error when generating code for a model containing Simulink function or Graphical function in Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might crash or produce an error when generating code for a model containing a Stateflow chart under the following circumstances: The Stateflow chart has a Simulink function exported from Stateflow. The Stateflow chart contains a graphical function.  When generating code, you may get this code generation error: ValidCore violated: Failed assertion: fcnType->numInputs() == nInputs || (fcnType->isVariadic() && fcnType->numInputs()".   MATLAB might crash. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:[ 0] 0x00007ff8bd15fd64 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+06356324 RTWCG::RTWContext::getModule+00000020  [ 1] 0x00007ff8bcc5924a C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+01086026 CG::GenericTagCategory<CG::RTWCGMatlabFcnInplaceTag,0>::GenericTagCategory<CG::RTWCGMatlabFcnInplaceTag,0>+00000714
Workaround: For Simulink function, set the Function Packaging parameter to Inline and for Graphical function, set the Function Inline option to?Inline.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: When distributed pipelining is enabled, models with a Divide block get "division by zero" warning in validation model
Description: When Distributed pipelining  is set to on  for a model that contains Divide blocks, during HDL code generation, pip eline delays are added in front of the Division block denominator input. With a default initial value of 0 from the delay bocks, these signals cause? "division by zero" warnings when you run the validation model generated during HDL code generation.  This warning happens only during the initial time steps of the validation model when the pipeline delay?are connected to the division blocks as inputs. After the?initial time steps, there are no more "division by zero" warnings. The generated HDL code is still valid and produces the same output as the original model after the?initial time steps.

Summary: Classes with dynamic properties that redefine dot indexing may be unable to access the dynamic properties with dot indexing
Description: If a class inherits from both the dynamicprops and matlab.mixin.indexing.RedefinesDot mixin classes, referencing or assigning to a dynamically added property using the dot indexing syntax dispatches to the overloaded indexing methods instead of retrieving or assigning to the dynamic property. For example, ExampleClass inherits from both mixins. classdef ExampleClass < dynamicprops & matlab.mixin.indexing.RedefinesDot ? ? methods (Access = protected) ? ? ? ? function out = dotReference(~,~) ? ? ? ? ? ? disp("in ExampleClass.dotReference"); ? ? ? ? ? ? out = 1; ? ? ? ? end?? ? ? ? function obj = dotAssign(obj, ~, ~) ? ? ? ? ? ? disp("in ExampleClass.dotAssign"); ? ? ? ? end?  ? ? ? ? function n = dotListLength(~,~,~) ? ? ? ? ? ? disp("in ExampleClass.dotListLength"); ? ? ? ? ? ? n = 1; ? ? ? ? end ? ? endend? Create an instance of ExampleClass and add a dynamic property, dynamicProp:?  >> obj = ExampleClass();>> addprop(obj, "dynamicProp"); Instead of retrieving the value of dynamicProp, MATLAB calls the overloaded indexing method ExampleClass.dotReference.? >> val = obj.dynamicProp;in ExampleClass.dotReference Similarly, instead of assigning to dynamicProp, MATLAB calls the overloaded assginment method ExampleClass.dotAssign.? >> obj.dynamicProp = 3;in ExampleClass.dotAssign?
Workaround: To overload dot indexing for classes that also inherit from the dynamicprops mixin, implement the subsref and/or subsasgn methods instead of inheriting from matlab.mixin.indexing.RedefinesDot.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Symbolic dimension is incorrectly inlined during model reference code generation
Description: The code generated for a model reference hierarchy might incorrectly inline the symbolic dimension variable for a signal under these conditions:The signal crosses model reference boundaries. One of the parent models in the model reference hierarchy is not connected to the data source (base workspace, Simulink data dictionary, or external source) used by the referenced model.?
Workaround: Explicitly link the parent model(s) to the data source, even if the objects in the source are not directly used by the model.

Summary: False positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 18.6 with static local variable
Description: Polyspace incorrectly raises a false positive violation of rule MISRA-C:2012 18.6 when the address of a static local variable is returned by a function.? For example: int *foo1(int *obj) {    static int *p;     p = obj;   // False positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 18.6    return p; } int main(void) {  static int *p1;   int *p2;    p2 = foo1(&p1); }

Summary: Unexpected code generated for a model when AdvancedOptControl is set to "-SLCI" and Simulink Code Inspector is not installed
Description: The generated code for a model might contain unexpected expressions when the model configuration parameter?AdvancedOptControl? is set to ' -SLCI ' and you do not have Simulink Code Inspector installed. The unexpected expressions result from optimizations that are disabled without Simulink Code Inspector installed. For example, the code might contain expressions adding 0 to the result: modelDW->Delay_DSTATE[(int_T)(idxDelay + 0)]  The unexpected code is functionally correct, but degrades the readability of the code.?
Workaround: When model configuration parameter AdvanceOptControl?is set to "-SLCI", check that Simulink Code Inspector is installed before generating code for your model.

Summary: MATLAB on?macOS 12 (Monterey) and later hangs while bluetoothlist scans for nearby Bluetooth Classic devices
Description: MATLAB installed on macOS 12 (Monterey) and later displays a busy icon and remains unresponsive?while scanning for nearby Bluetooth Classic devices using?bluetoothlist . However, you can still connect to any Bluetooth Classic?device by specifying its name or address.

Summary: MATLAB on Windows 11 hangs while connecting to a Bluetooth Low Energy device using?ble object
Description: MATLAB installed on Windows 11 displays a busy icon and remains unresponsive while connecting to a Bluetooth Low Energy device by using the?ble ?object.

Summary: Segmentation fault when Static Code Metrics are computed
Description: A crash might occur when the Embedded Coder or MATLAB Coder requests the Static Code Metrics. The stack trace points to a line in code_info.cpp .

Summary: Callback function specified by configureCallback?is sometimes?not executed
Description: A callback function specified as an input to the configureCallback ?method of the following?interface objects might not get triggered even when data is available to trigger it:serialport  tcpserver  tcpclient  udpport  bluetooth
Workaround: Use a MATLAB timer callback instead to implement similar behavior. For more information, see?timer.
Products: Instrument Control Toolbox, Industrial Communication Toolbox

Summary: RoadRunner fails to load Point Cloud files when a comma decimal point number locale is used
Description: During the process of preparing data to send to the PDAL library for loading and processing point clouds, RoadRunner encountered inconsistencies with some string conversions not aligning with PDAL's expectations. As a result, PDAL fails to parse the strings during Point Cloud load and prints an error saying "Invalid value for argument 'bounds'" in the output panel. This issue is more prominent on Linux.
Workaround: Switch number locale to period based decimal point (example: US English number locale) instead of a comma decimal point locale (example: German number locale).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for State Writer block writing to Outport block of reused conditionally executed subsystem
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for a model if these conditions exist: Model contains a State Writer block. State Writer block writes to an Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem (for example, an Enabled Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem). Model is configured with these model configuration parameter settings: System target file ?is set to 'ert.tlc'. Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  is set to Individual arguments .  For conditionally executed subsystem, block parameter?Function packaging ?is set to Reusable function . Conditionally executed subsystem is inside another nonvirtual subsystem for which block parameter?Function packaging ?set to Reusable function .  When these conditions are met, the generated code for the State Writer block might write to a global variable instead of writing to the argument variable passed in to the reusable function for the subsystem, causing an incorrect answer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Compiler error in Simulink when using custom code from different directories
Description: Simulink might report a compiler error when you simulate a model that uses multiple custom code files from different directories.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Modifying an instance of a value class in a standalone dialog might erroneously modify copy of object
Description: When you assign an instance of a value class?n1   to another variable n2  , modifying the value of n1   in a standalone dialog might also erroneously modify the value of n2  . Value classes include but are not limited to?Simulink.NumericType , Simulink.AliasType , and embedded.numerictype .
